headline: Result of AGM
brief: Primorus Investments PLC reports all AGM resolutions passed with strong shareholder support on 15 July 2026.
summary: All nine resolutions at Primorus Investments plc's AGM were approved. Resolution 1-5 and 7-9 received overwhelming backing with 47+ million votes in favour against minimal opposition (51,251-99,691 votes against). Resolution 6 showed lower support with 7.3 million votes for, 51,251 against, and 40.2 million withheld, suggesting potential shareholder concerns on that specific matter. Voting details were substantially identical to the Notice of AGM published 19 June 2026.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure showing standard AGM approval; Resolution 6's high abstention rate warrants attention but unlikely material market impact absent further context.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Avation repurchases 10,000 shares at 137.5p per share under approved buyback programme.
summary: Aircraft lessor Avation PLC repurchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at 137.5 pence per share, to be held in treasury. Following the transaction, 61,425,369 shares remain in issue with 1,580,068 treasury shares held, reducing voting rights to 59,845,301. The repurchase is conducted under the company's approved share buyback mandate authorising repurchase of up to 25% of issued capital, with authority valid until the next annual general meeting.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; modest share count reduction signals management confidence but minimal market impact given small buyback scale and ongoing fleet leasing business operations.

company: Hon Hai Precision Industry Co Ld
headline: Hyield obtaining Churchill Capital Corp XI Shares
brief: Hon Hai subsidiary Hyield invests $9m in Churchill Capital Corp XI common shares for long-term holding.
summary: Hon Hai's subsidiary Hyield Venture Capital acquired 900,000 common shares of Churchill Capital Corp XI at USD 10 per share on 15 July 2026, totalling USD 9 million. The investment represents 0.3% shareholding in Churchill Capital Corp XI with no restrictions on rights. The transaction was approved by Hon Hai's board and constitutes 0.14% of total assets and 0.3% of shareholder equity, classified as a long-term investment under the partnership agreement.
retail_take: Minor portfolio diversification by Hon Hai subsidiary with immaterial impact to consolidated financials; routine corporate investment activity unlikely to move markets significantly.

headline: NCC and Siemens strengthen critical infrastructure
brief: NCC Group and Siemens sign MOU to collaborate on UK critical infrastructure cyber security, combining OT and IT expertise.
summary: NCC Group and Siemens have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen cyber security across UK critical national infrastructure. The collaboration focuses on securing operational technology (OT) in industrial, energy, and defence sectors by combining Siemens' expertise in industrial automation and control systems with NCC Group's cyber security capabilities. The partnership targets the convergence of IT and OT environments where data systems connect with physical operations, aiming to support asset owners and supply chains with end-to-end cyber resilience solutions.
retail_take: This MOU represents a commercial opportunity for NCC Group in the growing critical infrastructure cyber security market, though it is non-binding and lacks specific financial terms or revenue commitments, making it a modest positive signal rather than material news.

headline: Director Declaration
brief: Metro Bank director Dorita Gilinski appointed non-executive director of GeoPark Limited effective 14 July 2026.
summary: Metro Bank Holdings PLC has disclosed that Dorita Gilinski, a non-executive director at the bank, has been appointed as a non-executive director of GeoPark Limited (NYSE: GPRK), effective 14 July 2026. The announcement was made in compliance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.9R(2), which requires listed companies to disclose director appointments at other organisations. This is a routine regulatory disclosure of an external directorship.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of director's external appointment; no material implications for Metro Bank's operations or strategy. Market likely to treat as non-event.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: FirstGroup CFO Ryan Mangold purchases 83 shares via company Share Incentive Plan at £1.81.
summary: Ryan Mangold, Executive Director and Chief Financial Officer of FirstGroup plc, purchased 83 ordinary shares of 5p each on 10 July 2026 at £1.8105 per share through the FirstGroup Share Incentive Plan, with a total value of £150.27. The purchase was made via Computershare Trustees Limited and notified on 15 July 2026 in compliance with Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014.
retail_take: Routine director share purchase under employee plan; modest transaction size suggests standard PDMR disclosure rather than material confidence signal.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Eight senior Prudential executives acquired shares through employee share purchase plan on 10 July at GBP 10.43 per share.
summary: Prudential PDMRs including CEO Anil Wadhwani, CFO Ben Bulmer, and other senior leaders acquired ordinary shares via the All Employee Share Purchase Plan on 10 July 2026. Total transactions: 272 shares purchased across eight executives at GBP 10.429195 per share. Acquisitions ranged from 36 to 40 shares per individual, representing routine insider share acquisitions at consistent pricing.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of insider share purchases through employee scheme showing management alignment with shareholders. Non-event; no material significance for equity investors as these represent standard employee benefit plan participation.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Dr. Martens AGM: all 20 resolutions passed with strong majorities on 15 July 2026.
summary: Dr. Martens held its AGM on 15 July 2026 with all 20 resolutions passing decisively. Ordinary resolutions (1-16) achieved 85-100% support, including approval of annual accounts, remuneration report, and final dividend. Director re-elections showed strong backing, with independent directors Robyn Perriss, Andrew Harrison, Ian Rogers, and Robert Hanson receiving 94.9-96.6% independent shareholder votes. Special resolutions authorising share allotment, pre-emption rights disapplication, share buybacks, and 14-day meeting notice all passed with 94.7-99.8% support. Voting participation reached 82.35% of issued capital.
retail_take: Routine disclosure with overwhelming shareholder endorsement across all resolutions, including board re-elections and capital management authorities. No contentious votes or governance concerns. Non-event for markets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hongkong Land repurchased 510,000 shares on 14 July at average price of US$7.12 per share; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Hongkong Land Holdings completed a share repurchase of 510,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from US$7.07 to US$7.25 per share, with a weighted average purchase price of US$7.1187. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, issued share capital comprises 2,134,890,826 ordinary shares with one vote each. The company holds no treasury shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management action. No material value signal given modest buyback size relative to total issued capital.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Costain completes first £10m tranche of share buyback; 504,928 shares bought at 204-210p this week.
summary: Costain Group purchased 504,928 ordinary shares between 8-14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 204p to 210p per share under its £20m buyback programme announced March 2026. Total shares bought to date: 5,064,190 for cancellation. Following settlement, issued shares will number 264,691,845. Panmure Liberum completed the first £10m tranche (excluding expenses); Investec Bank now commences the second £10m tranche from 15 July.
retail_take: Routine capital management executing announced policy; demonstrates capital discipline and supports per-share metrics. No major signals either way—buybacks at modest single-digit premium to book suggest confidence but market context unchanged.

headline: Dividend Timetable
brief: Cohort PLC announces final dividend timetable for year ended 30 April 2026, citing record revenue and adjusted operating profit.
summary: Cohort PLC's final dividend for the year ended 30 April 2026 will be payable on 2 October 2026 to shareholders on the register at 21 August 2026, subject to AGM approval on 24 September 2026. The announcement notes record revenue and adjusted operating profit ahead of expectations. The defence technology group operates seven businesses across communications, intelligence, sensors and effectors segments, employing over 1,700 staff globally.
retail_take: Positive signal: record profits and ahead-of-expectation performance support dividend continuation, though specific dividend amount and yield are not disclosed in this timetable announcement.

headline: Annual Financial Report
brief: Network Rail Infrastructure Finance reports full-year results for year ended 31 March 2026; £110k profit before tax.
summary: NRIF, the debt issuance vehicle for Network Rail, reported profit before tax of £110k (2025: £110k). Finance costs were £1,497m (2025: £1,416m), offset by finance income. The company made a £701m loss on fair value of debt due to interest rate movements, partially offset by £16m gain on derivatives. Net borrowings increased to £27,504m from £27,324m. UK RPI index-linked debt represents 92% of gross debt. Directors confirmed going concern, noting planned absorption of Network Rail into Great British Railways under the Railways Bill; the financial indemnity from the UK Government remains intact.
retail_take: Non-event for retail investors. NRIF is a pass-through financing vehicle with all costs recovered from Network Rail via intercompany loan. The £701m fair value loss is non-cash and accounting-driven; economic substance unchanged. Government guarantee provides implicit backstop.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Harwood Capital's stake in Avingtrans falls below 7% threshold after shareholding reduction.
summary: Harwood Capital LLP and Oryx International Growth Fund Limited, controlled by Christopher Harwood and Bernard Mills, reduced their combined voting rights in Avingtrans PLC from 7.911% to 6.282% (2.32m shares) as of 13 July 2026. The notification was filed on 15 July 2026 following a disposal of voting rights that crossed the major holdings threshold downward.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a stake reduction below the 7% threshold. No immediate implications for retail investors unless signaling broader fund strategy shifts.

headline: Post Stabilisation Notice Julius Baer 5yr Senior
brief: UBS confirms no stabilisation activity occurred during Julius Baer's €500m senior notes issuance.
summary: UBS AG issued a post-stabilisation notice on 15 July 2026 confirming that no stabilisation measures were undertaken by the stabilising manager during the offering of €500 million fixed rate senior unsecured notes issued through ELM B.V. on behalf of Julius Baer Group Ltd. The notes were not offered in the United States and remain unregistered under US securities law.
retail_take: Routine post-issuance disclosure confirming standard market practice; no stabilisation intervention suggests orderly order book at pricing, with minimal market impact implications.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: RM Infrastructure Income PLC director Norman Crighton tenders 11,945 shares in recent offer.
summary: Norman Crighton, Non-Executive Director of RM Infrastructure Income PLC, tendered 11,945 ordinary shares during a recent tender offer on 1 May 2026 at £0.7477 per share, totalling £8,931.28. His holding reduced from 29,982 shares to 18,037 shares, representing 0.03% of issued share capital. The transaction was conducted on the London Stock Exchange.
retail_take: Routine director dealing disclosure via tender offer; modest share reduction by non-executive; no material significance for investors.

headline: Notice of Half Year Results
brief: STV Group to publish H1 2026 results on 8 September with analyst presentation.
summary: STV Group plc will release its half-year results for the period ended 30 June 2026 on Tuesday, 8 September 2026. An analyst presentation will be hosted at 12:30pm via video conference by Chief Executive Rufus Radcliffe and Chief Financial & Operating Officer Lindsay Dixon. Interested parties should contact Angela Wilson at angela.wilson@stv.tv to attend.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of results publication date and presentation details; no material information released; market impact dependent on results content when published.

headline: Compliance with Market Abuse Regulation
brief: Invesco Bond Income Plus enters mandatory closed period ahead of half-year results announcement.
summary: Invesco Bond Income Plus Limited confirms all inside information held by directors has been disclosed to RNS, clearing the way for securities dealing. The company has entered a mandatory closed period under UK Market Abuse Regulation effective 15 July 2026, lasting until half-year results for the period ended 30 June 2026 are published. No new undisclosed inside information currently exists.
retail_take: Routine regulatory compliance disclosure signalling pre-results trading restrictions; standard practice for listed funds with no material implications.

headline: Amundi Physical Metals plc: UK Final Terms
brief: Amundi Physical Metals plc issues 35,000 additional gold ETC securities (Tranche 882), bringing total series to ~67m units.
summary: Amundi Physical Metals plc issued 35,000 ETC Securities on 15 July 2026 under Tranche 882 of its Amundi Physical Gold ETC programme. Metal entitlement per security: 0.03961782 fine troy ounces. Total series aggregate post-issuance: 66,983,037 units. Expense ratio: 0.12% per annum. Securities admitted to trading on LSE, Euronext Paris/Amsterdam, Deutsche Börse, Borsa Italiana, and Mexican Stock Exchange. Estimated net proceeds: USD 5.6m.
retail_take: Routine capital raise for established precious metals ETC with wide multi-exchange listing. Standard issuance mechanics; no material news for sentiment direction. Non-event for most retail investors.

headline: Publication of a Supplementary Prospectus
brief: Bank of America publishes supplementary prospectus incorporating Q2 2026 earnings disclosure for its $85bn Euro Medium-Term Note Program.
summary: Bank of America Corporation published a supplementary prospectus dated 15 July 2026, approved by the FCA, relating to its $85 billion Euro Medium-Term Note Program. The supplement incorporates by reference the issuer's Form 8-K filed 14 July 2026 containing earnings results for the three months ended 30 June 2026. The prospectus updates the base prospectus dated 13 May 2026 and excludes certain presentation materials and supplemental information from incorporation.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating debt prospectus with latest earnings; standard practice for ongoing note programs and immaterial for equities investors.

headline: Amundi Physical Metals plc: Final Terms
brief: Amundi Physical Metals issues 35,000 gold-backed ETC securities (Tranche 882) with 0.12% annual fee.
summary: Amundi Physical Metals plc (GLDA) issued 35,000 ETC securities on 15 July 2026 as Tranche 882 of its Amundi Physical Gold ETC programme. The securities carry a Metal Entitlement of 0.03961782 fine troy ounces and a Total Expense Ratio of 0.12% per annum. Total ETC securities outstanding post-issuance: 66,983,037. Authorised Participants include HSBC, Jane Street, Flow Traders, Optiver, BNP Paribas, and Virtu Financial. Securities are listed on Euronext Paris, Amsterdam, Deutsche Börse, Borsa Italiana, LSE, and Mexican Stock Exchange. Scheduled maturity: 23 May 2118.
retail_take: Routine capital raising announcement for established gold ETC product. Tranche issuance is standard operational activity with no material disclosure implications for investors.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Touchstar CEO Lynden Jones purchases 50,000 shares, increasing stake to 6.3% of issued voting capital.
summary: On 14 July 2026, Lynden Jones, Chief Executive Officer of Touchstar plc (AIM: TST), purchased 50,000 ordinary shares in four tranches at prices between 80.4 and 81.6 pence per share, totalling £40,281. Following the purchase, Jones now holds 500,000 shares representing approximately 6.3% of the Company's issued voting share capital. Touchstar is a logistics software and operational infrastructure provider serving industrial sectors including transport, warehousing, and fuel distribution.
retail_take: CEO share purchase signals confidence in the business and aligns management interests with shareholders; modest positive signal but routine for executives to build stakes periodically.

headline: Overseas Regulatory Announcement
brief: Standard Chartered sets 29 July board meeting to approve H1 2026 results and interim dividend payment.
summary: Standard Chartered PLC will hold a Board Committee meeting on 29 July 2026 to approve half-year results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 and consider an interim dividend. Subject to approval, the dividend record date is set for 7 August 2026, with different times for UK and Hong Kong registers (10.00pm London time and 9.00am Hong Kong time respectively). Share transfers must be lodged by 4.30pm Hong Kong time on 6 August 2026 to qualify for dividend entitlement.
retail_take: Routine procedural announcement confirming interim dividend dividend timetable; no material substance regarding dividend amount, rate, or earnings guidance disclosed.

headline: Admission to Trading of Transferable Securities
Standard form for notification of major holdings
brief: Ronald Lansdell's holding in Jersey Oil and Gas falls below notifiable threshold to 2.97%.
summary: Ronald Lansdell, subject to notification obligations, crossed a voting rights threshold on 13 July 2026, with his stake in Jersey Oil and Gas PLC reducing to 2.97% (970,601 voting rights). This represents a decrease from the previous notification level of 3.27%. The issuer was notified on 14 July 2026. The holding falls below the 3% notifiable threshold, triggering this standard disclosure requirement under DTR5 rules.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholder stake reduction; no material implications for equity valuation or company direction absent other context.

headline: Directorate Change
brief: Standard Chartered appoints Manus Costello as Group Chief Financial Officer, effective immediately, following regulatory approval.
summary: Standard Chartered PLC has confirmed the appointment of Manus Costello as Group Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director with immediate effect following receipt of regulatory approvals. The appointment was initially announced on 17 May 2026. Costello joins the board of Standard Chartered PLC, which operates in 54 markets globally with a dual listing on London and Hong Kong exchanges.
retail_take: Routine executive appointment disclosure confirming previously-announced GCFO hire with regulatory clearance obtained; likely non-event for markets absent further context on Costello's background or strategy implications.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Vistry CEO Adam Daniels purchases 38,372 shares at £2.61 for ~£100k on 15 July 2026.
summary: Adam Daniels, Chief Executive Officer of Vistry Group PLC, purchased 38,372 ordinary shares of 50p each on 15 July 2026 at £2.606 per share via the London Stock Exchange, representing a total investment of £99,997.43. This is an initial notification of the transaction under PDMR shareholding disclosure requirements.
retail_take: CEO share purchase signals management confidence in the company's value at current levels; modest position size suggests cautious rather than aggressive conviction, likely viewed as mildly positive but not material.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: FIL Limited's RS Group stake falls below 5% threshold to 4.99% following share disposal on 13 July.
summary: FIL Limited notified on 14 July that its voting rights in RS Group PLC fell below the 5% disclosure threshold. The stake decreased from 5.00% (previous notification) to 4.99%, representing 23.5 million voting rights. The threshold was crossed on 13 July 2026 through an acquisition or disposal of voting rights. FIL Limited, a Bermuda-registered entity, holds the shares directly with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding falling marginally below the regulatory notification threshold; unlikely to signal strategic change given minimal percentage movement.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Helical executives awarded deferred shares as part of 2026 bonus scheme; awards vest in 2029.
summary: Helical PLC granted deferred share awards to two executive directors on 14 July 2026 following FY2026 bonuses. Matthew Bonning-Snook received 2,719 shares and James Moss (CFO) received 9,888 shares, valued at 193.24 pence per share (five-day average). The nil-cost options will vest on 14 July 2029, incentivising three-year executive retention.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of bonus deferral arrangements; demonstrates standard executive compensation governance but no material market implications for retail investors.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: Aberdeen Asian Income Fund declares 5.55p second interim dividend, targeting 6.30% annualised yield.
summary: Aberdeen Asian Income Fund Limited declared a second quarterly interim dividend of 5.55p per share for year-end 31 December 2026, payable 21 August 2026 to shareholders registered 24 July 2026 (ex-dividend 23 July). Based on NAV of 355.08p on 30 June 2026, this represents 1.5625% of NAV per quarter, equating to 6.30% annualised yield. The fund achieved 30.9% share price total return over the six-month period to 30 June 2026.
retail_take: Solid dividend maintenance with attractive yield and strong recent performance suggests fund is delivering on income objectives; likely viewed positively by yield-focused investors.

headline: Exercise of Share Options, Issue of Equity and TVR
brief: ADF issuing 2.5m shares via options exercise; total shares increase to 110.3m by 20 July.
summary: Facilities by ADF plc will admit 2,500,000 ordinary shares (£0.01 each) to AIM trading on 20 July 2026 to satisfy employee share option exercises. Post-admission, the company will have 110,322,776 ordinary shares in issue with no treasury holdings. The new shares rank pari passu with existing shares. This figure serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations for shareholders.
retail_take: Routine equity issuance through option exercises. Dilutive but expected event; shareholders should note the new share count for notification threshold purposes.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: BH Macro repurchases 3,794 Sterling shares at 4.2350p; to hold in treasury.
summary: BH Macro Limited purchased 3,794 ordinary Sterling shares on 14 July 2026 via JP Morgan Securities at weighted average price of 4.2350 per share. Shares will be held in treasury. Following the transaction, Sterling shares in issue (excluding treasury) total 300,190,464 with 77,565,248 held in treasury. Total voting rights in the company stand at 459,514,370 rounded up.
retail_take: Routine share buyback with modest volume; treasury holdings reduce share count without capital cancellation, potentially supporting EPS. Market likely views as non-event or mildly accretive depending on discount to NAV.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Artemis UK Future Leaders bought back 12,000 shares at 366.6p; now holds 20.7m shares in treasury.
summary: On 14 July 2026, Artemis UK Future Leaders Plc purchased 12,000 ordinary shares (0.02% of issued capital) at 366.583 pence per share for treasury. The company's issued capital comprises 49,826,436 shares with 20,694,955 held in treasury, leaving 29,131,481 shares with voting rights. The announcement updates the voting rights denominator for disclosure threshold calculations under FCA rules.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury; modest capital management action with no material strategic implications. Standard disclosure with minimal market impact.

headline: Announcement Cancellation & Deletion of Own Shares
brief: OTE cancels 9.8m own shares following shareholder approval, reducing share capital by €27.7m.
summary: Hellenic Telecommunications Organization S.A. has cancelled 9,799,155 treasury shares acquired between May 2025 and April 2026 under approved buyback programmes. Share capital reduces from €1.14bn to €1.12bn, with total registered shares decreasing from 403.9m to 394.1m at €2.83 nominal value each. The cancellation takes effect on Euronext Athens on July 17, 2026, following Ministry of Development approval on July 2.
retail_take: Share cancellation is typically neutral to mildly positive, reflecting capital management and EPS accretion, though market impact depends on buyback execution pricing versus current valuations.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Orbit Capital appoints two new directors with debt capital markets and financial strategy expertise to boards and treasury committee.
summary: Orbit Capital PLC and Orbit Treasury Limited appointed Cécile Montfort and John Miesner as Company Directors effective 1 July 2026. Both join as Independent Committee members on Orbit's Treasury Committee. Montfort brings 20+ years in debt capital markets and risk analysis from roles at Crédit Agricole CIB, Moody's and Société Générale. Miesner, currently Global Head of Debt & Capital Advisory at Interpath Advisory, has extensive experience in debt, capital structure and financial strategy across residential property sector.
retail_take: Positive signal: experienced debt and capital markets expertise strengthens governance as housing association pursues long-term financial resilience and 2030 customer experience targets.

headline: New Share Buyback Programme
brief: B.P. Marsh launches new £2m share buyback programme managed by Singer Capital Markets, replacing previous scheme.
summary: B.P. Marsh & Partners (AIM: BPM) has initiated a new share buyback programme with a maximum consideration of £2 million, replacing an earlier programme that repurchased 47,000 shares for £0.3m before the July AGM. Singer Capital Markets will manage the non-discretionary programme independently, with shares repurchased in open market transactions at prices capped at 105% of the five-day average middle market quotation. Repurchased shares will be held in treasury for later reissue or cancellation, with the programme operating under shareholder authority to purchase up to 3.71m shares, expiring July 2027.
retail_take: Standard capital management measure signalling confidence in valuation; buybacks typically viewed as neutral-to-positive but modest scale (£2m) suggests no major share undervaluation concerns.

headline: MARKET ANNOUNCEMENT OF CHANGES TO CUSTODIANS OF EXCHANGE TRADED PRODUCT
MoU for sale of 50 MWp for EUR 12 million
brief: Zenith Energy signs MoU to sell 50 MWp Italian solar portfolio for EUR 12m, implying EUR 7m gross profit.
summary: Zenith Energy's Italian subsidiary WESOLAR has signed a non-binding MoU to sell a 50 MWp solar development portfolio in Piedmont to an international renewable energy operator for EUR 12 million. The portfolio has an aggregate acquisition and development cost of approximately EUR 5 million, yielding a gross profit of EUR 7 million (140% uplift). This represents the first potential monetisation of Zenith's 193 MWp Italian solar portfolio and validates the company's build-and-sell strategy at EUR 240,000 per MWp versus EUR 100,000 per MWp acquisition cost.
retail_take: Positive signal of asset monetisation capability and portfolio valuation, though deal remains non-binding subject to due diligence and definitive documentation. Proceeds intended for reinvestment in expanded portfolio.

headline: SEGRO CAPITALISES ON STRONG LEASING MOMENTUM
brief: SEGRO secures three major leases totalling 540,000 sq ft at Coventry Park, adding £6m annual rent.
summary: SEGRO signed leases with Volvo Group UK (91,000 sq ft build-to-suit, operational early 2027), DIRKS Consumer Logistics (306,000 sq ft pre-let, completing H1 2027), and GigaCloud Technology (140,500 sq ft speculative unit). The lettings generate £6m new annual rent, included within the £53m headline rent secured in H1 2026. SEGRO Park Coventry will deliver 3.7m sq ft across 73 acres with nearly 1.7m sq ft now leased; units target BREEAM Excellent and EPC A ratings, creating up to 5,000 jobs upon full occupation.
retail_take: Strong momentum in big-box logistics leasing demonstrates sustained demand for high-quality, well-located industrial space and validates SEGRO's development strategy. Market likely reads positively as evidence of rental growth and portfolio quality.

headline: First JORC Reserve declared, JORC Resource updated
brief: Savannah Resources declares first JORC ore reserve of 20Mt at 0.99% Li2O for Barroso lithium project in Portugal.
summary: Savannah Resources announced its maiden JORC Probable Ore Reserve of 20Mt at 0.99% Li2O for the Barroso Lithium Project in Portugal, underpinning a 14-year Phase 1 production profile. The reserve is based on conservative lithium concentrate pricing of US$1,200/t versus current spot prices around US$2,250/t. Total JORC resources remain at 39.2Mt at 1.05% Li2O, with additional upside from 12.3Mt inferred material and 35-62Mt exploration target. The project is designated a 'Strategic Project' under the EU Critical Raw Materials Act and received Portuguese state development grant approval of €110m.
retail_take: Positive milestone for project de-risking and financing progress; reserve declaration validates feasibility study economics and provides investor confidence for development, though conservative pricing assumptions suggest further upside potential.

headline: Barroso Li Project: Definitive Feasibility Study
brief: Barroso Lithium Project Phase 1 DFS confirms strong economics with 43% post-tax IRR and €110m Portuguese grant backing.
summary: Savannah Resources released Phase 1 Definitive Feasibility Study for its Barroso Lithium Project in Portugal showing 14-year mine life producing 2.56Mt spodumene concentrate at 183ktpa. Base case unlevered post-tax NPV of US$913m, IRR 43%, payback 1.9 years, and US$3.2bn EBITDA. C1 operating costs US$473/t and AISC US$646/t position project in second quartile globally. Initial CAPEX US$377m (net US$283m after Portuguese State Grant). First strategic partnership with AMG Critical Materials includes 45ktpa offtake and potential €270m German government loan guarantee. Construction targeted for 2027.
retail_take: Strong feasibility metrics and secured European government backing de-risk project development; favourable lithium market dynamics and EU strategic material demand support investment case, though execution and financing completion remain milestones.

company: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust Plc
headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: City of London Investment Management Company Limited crosses 5% voting rights threshold in BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust.
summary: City of London Investment Management Company Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of City of London Investment Group plc, notified on 14 July 2026 of crossing the 5% voting rights threshold in BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc (ISIN GB00B01RDH75). The notification relates to an acquisition of voting rights effective 10 July 2026. The entity now holds 4,545,543 voting rights representing 5.02% of the issuer, all held indirectly through shares with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major holding notification. No material implications for shareholders; standard FCA TR-1 filing following threshold breach by a fund management subsidiary.

headline: Launch of Retail Offer
brief: Investment Company PLC launches retail offer of 392,615 shares at 76.41p, with admission expected 28 July.
summary: Investment Company PLC is offering 392,615 new ordinary shares (nominal value £0.01 each) to UK retail investors at 76.41 pence per share via BookBuild, representing a 2% discount to NAV as at 14 July 2026. The retail offer opens 15 July and closes 23 July 2026, with admission to London Stock Exchange expected 28 July. Combined with an earlier institutional placing and offer for subscription announced 10 July, the total fundraising raises approximately £7.16 million. Proceeds will fund investments in quoted UK small and mid-cap companies and alternative assets including Bitcoin. Minimum subscription is £100 per investor.
retail_take: Modest-sized capital raise with retail inclusion after institutional round; 2% NAV discount and low minimum suggest inclusive positioning, though Bitcoin exposure adds volatility risk. Market neutral event typical of investment company secondary fundraisings.

headline: Viuda Sampling Indicates Increased Mineralisation
brief: GSC identifies expanded Au-Ag-Cu mineralisation at Viuda Negra prospect covering 500x700m area with potential for further extension.
summary: Great Southern Copper's rock and channel chip sampling at the Viuda Negra prospect has defined anomalous Au-Ag-Cu mineralisation across a 500x700 metre area, with high-grade silver-copper outcrops up to 186g/t Ag and 1.4% Cu in epithermal silica ledges. The mineralised system remains open to the west and south beneath gravel cover, extending the intermediate argillic alteration zone 800m further south from 2025 scout drilling that discovered Maricunga-style porphyry gold mineralisation. Planning and permitting for the next drilling phase are underway, targeting mineralisation beneath shallow cover.
retail_take: Positive indicator of exploration progress expanding the mineralisation footprint beyond initial scout drilling; positive step toward de-risking the project ahead of planned drilling, though exploration-stage companies carry inherent risk.

headline: Retail Offer
brief: Supermarket Income REIT launches retail share offer via RetailBook platform for UK investors; £216m acquisition pipeline planned.
summary: Supermarket Income REIT (LSE: SUPR) is conducting a conditional retail offer of new ordinary shares at a price to be determined, open to UK-based existing and new investors with a £250 minimum subscription. Proceeds fund purchases of three supermarkets (Sainsbury's Manchester, Tesco Edinburgh and Halifax) for approximately £118m and a further six UK grocery assets valued at £98m. Offer closes 3pm 15 July 2026, conditional on shareholder approval at 3 August general meeting, with admission expected 5 August 2026.
retail_take: Democratising access to primary market investment for retail investors reflects FCA rule changes; acquisition of established supermarket assets suggests stable income generation, though capital at risk given property/retail sector cyclicality.

headline: Commencement of Share Buyback Programme
brief: Barratt Redrow commences £386m share buyback programme, with first tranche of £190m to run through end-2026.
summary: Barratt Redrow PLC is initiating a share buyback programme worth up to £386m (excluding stamp duty and expenses) to reduce share capital, with purchased shares to be cancelled. Barclays Bank will manage the first tranche of £190m, commencing immediately and ending no later than 31 December 2026. The full buyback programme runs until 2 July 2027. The programme operates under shareholder authority granted at the November 2025 AGM and complies with FCA Listing Rules and market abuse regulations.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and provide shareholder returns; the £386m commitment demonstrates capital strength post-results, though execution risk depends on market conditions.

headline: Publication of Circular & Notice of GM
brief: Zotefoams convening general meeting on 3 August 2026 to approve new SAYE employee share plan.
summary: Zotefoams has published a circular and notice convening a general meeting for 3 August 2026 at 10:00am in London to approve ordinary resolution adopting the Zotefoams Save As You Earn Plan (SAYE Plan) and authorise directors to implement it. Directors unanimously recommend shareholder approval. Proxy votes must be received by 30 July 2026. The announcement follows strong FY2025 results showing record revenue of £158.5m (up 7%) and adjusted operating profit of £22.8m (up 26%).
retail_take: Routine corporate governance disclosure establishing employee share scheme. Non-event for equity valuation; standard practice for listed companies to broaden employee share ownership.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: FIL Limited crosses 10% threshold in Johnson Service Group with 11.41% stake via shares and CFDs.
summary: FIL Limited, a Bermuda-registered entity, notified on 14 July 2026 that it crossed a major holding threshold in Johnson Service Group PLC on 13 July 2026. FIL Limited now holds 11.41% of voting rights, comprising 9.95% through direct share ownership (37.01m shares) and 1.46% via cash-settled CFDs (5.43m voting rights). The previous notification showed 11.43%, indicating a marginal decrease. The holding was completed in Dublin on 14 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of major shareholding change. FIL Limited (Fidelity group) maintains significant but stable stake; minimal movement suggests portfolio adjustment rather than strategic intent.

headline: Exercise of Options
brief: Directors exercise 5m options for £130k; former director exercises 5m options for £170k. 10m new shares admitted to LSE.
summary: Cobra Resources directors Daniel Maling and David Clarke exercised options totalling 5m shares on 14 July 2026, paying £130k combined (2m shares at 2p, 3m at 3p). Clarke's stake increases to 11.03% ownership (119.7m shares). Former non-executive director Greg Hancock separately exercised 5m options for £170k (3m at 3p, 2m at 4p). All 10m new shares admitted to LSE on 17 July 2026. Post-admission, total voting rights: 1,085,653,414 shares. Cobra is a South Australian rare earths and copper explorer focused on the Boland ionic rare earth project.
retail_take: Management and former director commitment to the company through option exercises is typically viewed positively, though dilution of 10m shares is material. Clarke's increased stake signals confidence. Routine capital event with modest positive undertones.

headline: Director/PDMR Dealing
brief: Non-Executive Director Helen Gilder purchases 5,409 shares at 92.35p, holding 0.01% of voting rights.
summary: Helen Gilder, Non-Executive Director of Celebrus Technologies PLC (AIM: CLBS), purchased 5,409 ordinary shares of 2 pence each on 14 July 2026 at 92.35 pence per share on the London Stock Exchange. Following this transaction, her total beneficial holding stands at 5,409 shares, representing 0.01% of the company's voting rights. Celebrus is an AIM-listed data solutions provider offering first-party data capture, fraud prevention, and analytics platforms across financial services, retail, healthcare and other sectors.
retail_take: Routine director dealing disclosure with minimal shareholding; modest buy-in by board member suggests modest confidence but immaterial in size and impact on company structure.

headline: Fact Sheet Announcement
brief: RECI publishes June 2026 fact sheet showing NAV increase to 140.6p amid portfolio growth.
summary: Real Estate Credit Investments Ltd's June 2026 fact sheet reveals a diversified portfolio of 23 investments valued at £282.9m with £16.5m available cash and 29.0% net leverage. NAV rose from 139.3p in May to 140.6p in June, driven by 0.9p interest income, 0.5p asset valuations (primarily UK student accommodation uplift), 0.1p FX gains, offset by 0.2p expenses. Cash Equivalents held £8.2m in Money Market Funds.
retail_take: Routine monthly disclosure showing steady NAV progression with modest gains; positive valuation movements in core student accommodation assets suggest portfolio stabilisation, though incremental monthly gains reflect normal operational performance.

headline: CFO succession and NED appointment
brief: Convatec CFO Fiona Ryder to step down; Tobias Hestler appointed as Non-Executive Director.
summary: Fiona Ryder, CFO since November 2025, will stand down once a successor is appointed. She joined Convatec in January 2022 and led significant initiatives including a $500m bond issuance and first share buyback, reducing G&A expenses from 13% to below 7% of sales. Tobias Hestler, former Haleon Group CFO (2022-2024) and GSK Consumer Healthcare CFO, joins as NED effective immediately and will chair the Audit and Risk Committee after an orderly transition. Convatec expects H1 2026 results in line with expectations and double-digit adjusted EPS growth for 2026, with 5-7% organic revenue growth and ≥23% adjusted operating margin.
retail_take: Orderly planned succession with strong incoming NED pedigree suggests continuity; outgoing CFO's efficiency gains (G&A halved) provide positive context, but market may await successor appointment clarity and monitor any transition risks.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: BlackRock crosses notification threshold, now holding 6.22% of Caledonia Mining.
summary: BlackRock, Inc. notified Caledonia Mining Corporation on July 14, 2026 that it crossed a relevant change threshold on July 13, 2026. BlackRock's total position stands at 6.22% of voting rights (4.92% through direct shares of 952,194 voting rights, 0.89% via securities lending, and 0.40% via CFDs), down slightly from 6.17% previously. The notification follows standard regulatory requirements under AIM Rules for Companies.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder position change; no material implications for share price or company strategy. BlackRock's holding decreased marginally, suggesting portfolio rebalancing rather than strategic shift.

headline: Portfolio Company Update -Vesari Inc
brief: Vesari, Tekcapital's fifth portfolio company, files eleven geothermal-powered AI data centre patents and explores public markets listing.
summary: Vesari Inc., 51%-owned by Tekcapital (AIM: TEK), has filed eleven non-provisional US patent applications covering integrated geothermal power, cooling, workload orchestration, LEO satellite connectivity, and carbon-free energy verification for hyperscale AI data centres. The patents protect a closed-loop efficiency system rather than single innovations. Vesari is simultaneously exploring preliminary discussions with US investment bankers regarding a potential reverse merger or de-SPAC transaction with concurrent financing to access capital markets and fund commercialisation.
retail_take: Patent filing marks operational progress for an early-stage, high-risk venture in a buoyant AI infrastructure sector; de-SPAC discussions are exploratory only. Material upside depends on patent grants and transaction completion, both uncertain.

headline: EMTN Programme: Publication of Final Terms
brief: Pennon Group's South West Water Finance issues £350m green bonds: £250m fixed-rate notes and £100m inflation-linked notes.
summary: South West Water Finance Plc issued £250m of 6.000% fixed-rate notes due July 2035 (rated Baa1/A-) priced at 98.836%, yielding 6.172%, plus £100m of 3.806% RPI-linked notes due July 2041 at par. Both are senior unsecured, guaranteed by South West Water Limited, and designated green bonds for eligible projects under Pennon's Sustainable Financing Framework. Net proceeds approximately £345.76m. Notes listed on LSE and restricted to professional investors only.
retail_take: Material refinancing/capital raise for water utility at investment-grade ratings. 6% coupon reflects current gilt yields and utility credit profile; inflation linkage on second tranche hedges RPI risk. Positive for yield-hungry institutional investors; no retail distribution. Non-event for equity holders absent liquidity concerns.

headline: Dividend Declaration & Trading Update
brief: M Winkworth declares 3.3p interim dividend; H1 trading resilient despite economic uncertainty.
summary: M Winkworth, the London-based residential real estate franchisor, declared an interim dividend of 3.3p per ordinary share payable 19 August 2026. H1 2026 sales remained resilient and lettings proved resilient following the May 2026 Renters Rights Act implementation. The company opened four offices and closed one in the period. Directors expect FY2026 revenues and pre-tax profits in line with analyst consensus (£10.2m revenue, £2.4m pre-tax profit), with underlying revenues slightly ahead of prior year excluding the Crystal Palace office disposal impact.
retail_take: Positive signal: resilient operational performance and maintained dividend despite acknowledged political/economic uncertainty and recent uneven trading trends suggest underlying business stability.

headline: Royalty Interest  - Garfield Project
brief: Sunrise Resources notes exploration progress at Garfield project where it holds 2% NSR royalty interest.
summary: Guardian Metal Resources announced geological discoveries at its Garfield Copper-Gold-Silver Project in Nevada's Walker Lane, identifying epithermal precious metal structures in the High Grade zone and a new Mother zone with stockwork veining. GMR staked additional claims for the Mother zone, which lies wholly within Sunrise's royalty area, as does approximately half the High Grade zone. Sunrise retains a 2% NSR royalty over the original claim area and 1-mile surrounding zone, requiring no capital contribution but entitled to revenue-based payments.
retail_take: Positive signal for Sunrise: exploration success on royalty asset increases value potential while maintaining zero development risk and free-carry structure. Market likely reads this as de-risking of royalty holding.

headline: Q3 2026 Corporate Presentation
brief: Great Western Mining publishes Q3 2026 corporate presentation highlighting Nevada tungsten corridor strategy alongside copper and precious metals portfolio.
summary: Great Western Mining Corporation PLC (AIM: GWMO) released its Q3 2026 corporate presentation on 15 July 2026, available on its website. The presentation outlines the company's strategy to advance an emerging tungsten corridor in Nevada, positioning it to benefit from strengthening fundamentals for this critical mineral. The company continues advancing its Huntoon Copper Project, which holds a JORC-compliant resource of 4.3 Mt at 0.45% Cu with porphyry upside potential, plus gold and silver exposure through exploration and tailings reprocessing opportunities.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of quarterly presentation materials with no material new financial information; market likely views this as a non-event containing strategy already communicated, though tungsten positioning aligns with broader critical minerals demand trends.

headline: New Loan Facility and Operational Update
brief: Kropz secures ZAR 200m loan from related party UBI to fund working capital as Elandsfontein mine faces operational headwinds.
summary: Kropz's South African Elandsfontein phosphate mine secured a ZAR 200 million (~US$12.3m) loan from Ubunto-Botho Investments (UBI), a related party, to support operational cashflow. Q2 2026 production fell 17% to 95,956 tonnes versus prior quarter due to ore body variability, contractor availability issues, and geopolitical cost pressures. Sales totalled 183,714 tonnes with inventory at ~94,000 tonnes. The loan carries interest at South African prime plus 6% with no fixed maturity, repayable on demand with 15 business days' notice. UBI holds controlling stake in ARC, which previously provided financing.
retail_take: Negative signal: repeated funding rounds and production declines suggest ongoing operational strain and cash burn, raising questions about project viability and shareholder dilution risk.

headline: Notice of Half Year Results
brief: Canal+ to announce H1 2026 results on 28 July with analyst webcast.
summary: Canal+ S.A will publish its half-year results for the six months ending 30 June 2026 on Tuesday, 28 July 2026 at 08:30 BST. An analyst and investor webcast will follow at 09:30 CET. The global media and entertainment company operates across 70+ countries with 40+ million subscribers, spanning pay-TV, streaming, production, and distribution through divisions including MultiChoice, STUDIOCANAL, and Dailymotion.
retail_take: Routine results notification; market reaction will depend entirely on H1 2026 financial performance and forward guidance when released, not the announcement itself.

headline: Block Listing Return
brief: Fever-Tree reports block listing return for employee share schemes covering December 2025 to July 2026.
summary: Fever-Tree issued 79,660 shares under its Long Term Incentive Plan and 47,468 shares under its Savings Related Share Option Scheme during the period 2 December 2025 to 14 July 2026. Remaining unallotted securities total 934,286 under the LTIP and 41,648 under the SAYE scheme. No increases to scheme limits were applied for during the period.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of employee share scheme activity with modest allotments and no material capital implications. Standard governance reporting with no operational or strategic significance.

headline: Notice of Results
brief: LondonMetric Property to announce half-year results on 24 November 2026 with analyst presentation and webcast.
summary: LondonMetric Property PLC, a FTSE 100 Triple Net Lease REIT, will release half-year results for the six months ending 30 September 2026 on Tuesday 24 November 2026. An in-person analyst presentation begins at 08:30am with live webcast access available. The FTSE 100 real estate company focuses on high-quality, long-leased portfolio delivering reliable income with 11 years of dividend progression, 98% occupancy, and the sector's lowest cost ratio at 7.7%.
retail_take: Routine notice scheduling results announcement; no material new information. Market will await actual half-year results on 24 November for substantive trading implications.

headline: Notice of AGM
brief: Odyssean Investment Trust announces 2026 AGM scheduled for 10 September at London venue.
summary: Odyssean Investment Trust PLC's Annual General Meeting will be held in person on 10 September 2026 at 6 Stratton Street, London. The Board and Odyssean Capital LLP representatives will attend. Shareholders can submit questions in advance by 3 September to OIT@nsm.group. Voting will be conducted by poll with the Chair voting according to proxies held. Results will be published via RIS announcement and the company website immediately following the meeting.
retail_take: Routine corporate governance disclosure for shareholder meeting. In-person format and advance question submission represent standard AGM procedures with no material implications.

headline: Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
brief: Franchise Brands announces investor presentation on 3 August covering interim results via Investor Meet Company platform.
summary: CEO Peter Molloy and CFO Neil Miller will present Franchise Brands' interim results on 3 August 2026 at 11:30 BST through Investor Meet Company, open to all shareholders. The AIM-listed franchise platform operates 7 brands across 10 countries with ~600 franchisees, 2,500 mobile service vehicles, and conducts ~1.3m jobs annually at £325 average value, earning 15% management fees from franchisees. Pre-event questions accepted until 2 August 09:00 BST.
retail_take: Routine investor engagement disclosure. Presentation of interim results is standard practice for listed companies; no material information disclosed, though upcoming results announcement may influence sentiment depending on trading performance.

headline: Notice of AGM, Fundraising and Board Changes
brief: R8 Capital raises £500k via share placing and subscription to fund reverse takeover search.
summary: R8 Capital conditionally raised £500,000 gross through issuing 427,350,425 new ordinary shares at £0.00117 per share, subject to AGM approval on 7 August 2026. The company will use net proceeds to search for a reverse takeover target after deploying ~£225,000 to settle trading liabilities, achieving debt-free status. A share sub-division from £0.01 to £0.0001 nominal value enables the discounted pricing. Current Executive Chairman Jonathan Rowland and Executive Director Richard Morecroft will resign post-AGM, with David Mason joining as Executive Director and at least one Non-Executive Director appointed.
retail_take: Typical shell company recapitalisation ahead of acquisition; management changes and capital raise are pre-deal positioning. Market sentiment likely neutral-to-mildly-positive if investors view Mason's financial services experience as credible acquisition-execution capability, though execution risk remains high for reverse takeovers.

headline: Appointment of Corporate Broker
brief: Schroder AsiaPacific Fund PLC appoints Panmure Liberum Limited as sole corporate broker and financial adviser, effective immediately.
summary: Schroder AsiaPacific Fund plc has appointed Panmure Liberum Limited as its corporate broker and financial adviser with immediate effect from 15 July 2026. The appointment replaces previous arrangements and provides the investment trust with dedicated financial advisory and broking support. Panmure Liberum will serve as sole broker going forward.
retail_take: Routine administrative change signalling continuity of governance arrangements. Market likely treats as non-event unless prior broker change was disputed.

headline: Notice to Eligible Warrant Holders
brief: Hamak Strategy offers warrant holders exchange deal: 5 warrants for 1 new share, targeting 80% dilution reduction.
summary: Hamak Strategy Limited is conducting a voluntary warrant exchange offer for holders of 0.8p warrants, exchanging five warrants for one new ordinary share. If all 452.1 million eligible warrants participate, approximately 90.4 million new shares would be issued, reducing potential future dilution by ~80% versus full warrant exercise. The offer requires minimum acceptance of 100 million warrants and remains open for 10 business days from 13 July 2026, with shares expected within one month of closure.
retail_take: Positive signal: addresses shareholder concerns about warrant overhang and simplifies capital structure without requiring cash outlay, though dilution still occurs if all warrants convert.

headline: Transaction Update
brief: ALT Resources' Tartana deal faces rival bid; board spill vote scheduled for 17 August 2026.
summary: ALT Resources is proceeding with its proposed transaction with Tartana Minerals Ltd, announced 28 May 2026. However, Tartana has received an unsolicited, non-binding strategic investment proposal from a third party. An extraordinary general meeting is scheduled for 17 August 2026 following a notice to remove directors, with a board spill vote planned. Tartana states there is no certainty the rival proposal will result in a binding transaction or constitute a superior offer. ALT remains engaged with Tartana and continues monitoring developments.
retail_take: Deal uncertainty increases with rival bidder and board instability at target company; typical M&A execution risk, market likely to view negatively as original transaction completion becomes less certain.

headline: Updated Settlement Agreements
brief: Technology Minerals updates debt restructuring terms with Jonathan Swann and Atlas, reducing near-term cash burden ahead of placing.
summary: Technology Minerals has amended settlement agreements with Jonathan Swann (£2.9m restructured amount) and Atlas Special Opportunities II (£1.7m restructured amount), both dated July 2026. Swann's deal converts £1.625m to 3.25bn shares at £0.0005 per share, with £1.275m remaining as a term loan. Atlas receives £750k cash (down from £1m), £300m in shares (600m shares), and £650k as an 8% loan repayable in 24 months. Both agreements reduce immediate cash obligations and remain conditional on completing the anticipated placing and FCA prospectus approval.
retail_take: Restructuring reduces near-term liquidity pressure and increases equity component, but massive dilution from 3.85bn new shares and extended debt maturity suggest continued financial stress and shareholder value erosion.

headline: Production, Operations, Corporate ,Finance Update
brief: Angus Energy reports Q2 2026 revenues up 37% to £7.16m driven by higher gas prices and production gains.
summary: Angus Energy's Q2 2026 production from Saltfleetby reached 530 million standard cubic feet of gas and 3,365 barrels of condensate, with gas sales of 5.85 million therms. Estimated revenues of £7.16m represent a 37% increase from Q1 2026 (£5.24m), driven by higher realised gas prices, improved hedging, and production uplift from successful well workovers. The company reduced senior debt with Trafigura to £24.7m following a £1.29m principal repayment. Brockham oil field maintained stable production at 44 barrels per day with 100% operational efficiency.
retail_take: Positive: Revenue growth, debt reduction, and operational improvements from workovers demonstrate improving fundamentals. Planned July maintenance downtime presents near-term production volatility but signals active field management and capex investment.

headline: Half Year Operational Update
brief: Cizzle's lung cancer blood test achieves US CLIA accreditation, marking transition to commercialisation phase with expanded royalty commitments.
summary: Cizzle Biotechnology's CIZ1B biomarker test achieved CLIA accreditation for clinical use in the US through OmniHealth Diagnostics in Dallas, enabling routine clinical practice. The company secured US and Canadian patents protecting its core measurement technology. Guaranteed minimum royalty receipts from North American partner BIO increased to approximately US$5.9 million. The board plans nationwide US rollout across Texas, New York, California, South Carolina, Tennessee and Florida, whilst pursuing additional licensing partnerships in UK, Europe and Asia-Pacific regions.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as validation of science and proof of commercialisation pathway, de-risking the business model; however, this is an operational update with no financial results or revenue guidance provided.

company: Gore Street Energy Storage Fund PLC
headline: Final Results
brief: GSF reports NAV decline to 74.9p from 102.8p; announces 7.19pps dividends including 3p special; plans asset sales and augmentation.
summary: NAV fell 27% to 74.9p per share (31 March 2026) from 102.8p year-ago, primarily driven by lower merchant revenue forecasts from third-party valuers reducing NAV by 19p. Operational capacity nearly doubled to 643MW; revenue grew to £36.27m despite lower per-MW rates from oversaturated markets. New Board announced revised strategy in March focusing on asset sales, cost-cutting, and reinvestment into augmentation works. Total dividends declared 7.19pps including 3p special; Q4 dividend set at 1.75pps. Company holds £51.6m cash, £37.8m undrawn debt; gearing at 21.9%.
retail_take: Significant NAV erosion driven by external valuation factors rather than operational deterioration creates headwinds. New management strategy and higher 7pps fixed dividend policy offer potential upside if asset sales and augmentation execute successfully, but near-term sentiment likely negative given valuation hit.

headline: Update on the Retirement Disposal
brief: RESI completes £1.9bn retirement housing portfolio sale to Living REIT; shareholders to receive 66.1m LIVE shares.
summary: Residential Secure Income has satisfied all conditions for its strategic disposal of retirement housing assets to Living REIT plc. The sale involves RHP Holdings Limited, which indirectly holds 1,907 senior living rental flats and 256 housing manager flats through The Retirement Housing Partnership. Completion occurs 16 July 2026 with allotment of 66,103,233 newly issued LIVE ordinary shares (£0.01 each) as initial consideration, to be distributed in specie to RESI shareholders following admission to the London Stock Exchange.
retail_take: Positive signal: successful completion of major portfolio exit at agreed terms with established housing REIT partner, reducing RESI complexity and enabling shareholder capital redistribution through LIVE stake.

headline: Half Year Trading Update
brief: Artisanal Spirits maintains H1 EBITDA despite lower trade cask sales; branded growth and cash improve.
summary: The Artisanal Spirits Company (AIM: ART) reports H1-26 group revenue broadly flat versus H1-25, with branded businesses (SMWS, Single Cask Nation, Artisan Casks, JGT) growing high single digits, offsetting lower trade cask sales weighted to H2. H1-26 EBITDA maintained versus prior year; net cash flow improved c£1.5m. SMWS membership at 39,000+ with 70% retention and 15% year-on-year recruitment growth. USA bottle sales up c10%, Australia double digits; EU online declined 10%. Board confident in full-year FY26 expectations.
retail_take: Branded momentum and improved cash generation offset cyclical trade cask weakness, with strong US/Asia performance and new member recruitment offsetting EU softness. Market likely views positively as strategic brands deliver growth and cash improves despite near-term headwinds.

headline: Board Changes
brief: Distil PLC announces board restructure: Don Goulding retires as Executive Chairman after 16 years; Philip Naughton becomes Non-Executive Chairman; David Smith appointed CEO.
summary: Don Goulding steps down from Distil's board effective 14 August 2026 after 16 years, during which he developed RedLeg Spiced Rum into a flagship brand and secured a new UK/international production partnership. Philip Naughton, who joined as non-executive director in February 2026, assumes Non-Executive Chairman role. David Smith, with 25+ years' drinks industry experience including CEO roles at Fielden Whisky and The Oxford Artisan Distillery, joins as CEO subject to due diligence completion. The restructuring separates executive and chairman functions as the company positions for growth.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively: experienced drinks executive replacing founder-leader suggests professional scaling up, while Naughton's financial services/governance background and new partnership provide credibility for next growth phase.

headline: Half-Year Report
brief: Audioboom reports record H1 with 30% revenue growth to $45.7m and 80% adjusted EBITDA growth to $3.2m.
summary: Audioboom Group (AIM: BOOM) delivered H1 2026 revenue of US$45.7 million (up 30% YoY) with adjusted EBITDA profit of US$3.2 million (up 80%). Showcase advertising marketplace surged 60% to US$18.6 million. Monthly downloads/views reached 183 million in Q2 (up 84%). Company rejected three acquisition proposals during strategic review, deeming them undervalued. Cash position strengthened to US$5.4 million with US$10 million revolving credit facility pending. Booked revenue for 2026 exceeds US$81 million, already surpassing full-year 2025 total of US$80.4 million.
retail_take: Strong operational leverage and rejection of takeover offers signals management confidence in standalone growth trajectory. US$81m booked revenue ahead of schedule, pending credit facility, and Spotify/Apple partnerships support positive market narrative.

headline: Update on Comet Litigation
brief: US Court of Appeals reverses 2022 judgment in Comet litigation, vacating $40m damages award and $17m legal fees.
summary: The US Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit reversed the District Court's 2022 judgment against XP Power in trade secret proceedings brought by Comet Technologies USA. The reversal vacates the previous award of approximately US$40m in compensatory and punitive damages, US$17m in attorney fees, and a permanent injunction. The case has been remanded for a new trial, with cash from these awards currently held in a bond with the District Court. XP Power will continue defending its position and reports interim results on 4 August 2026.
retail_take: Significantly positive development; removal of substantial financial liability and injunction reduces near-term risk and uncertainty, though ultimate outcome of retrial remains uncertain.

headline: Second quarter production results
brief: Rio Tinto reports H1 2026 copper equivalent production up 3% YoY; Oyu Tolgoi delivers 31% growth on schedule.
summary: Rio Tinto achieved 3% copper equivalent production growth in H1 2026 despite geopolitical headwinds. Oyu Tolgoi copper production surged 31% YoY, remaining on track for ~500kt annual output 2028-2036. Pilbara iron ore reached highest H1 production since 2018; lithium output rose 53% YoY. Copper C1 unit costs guidance reduced to 30-50c/lb from 65-75c/lb due to higher gold prices and productivity gains. However, Kennecott faced a smelter furnace breach impacting H2 production, and Oyu Tolgoi paid $443M in contested Mongolia tax assessments.
retail_take: Strong operational delivery and cost guidance improvements are offset by tax disputes in Mongolia and Kennecott disruptions; mixed signals for near-term cash flow and earnings visibility.

headline: Gold Discovery In Nevada, USA
brief: London BTC discovers high-grade gold in Nevada with surface samples grading up to 16.23 g/t Au and 50.5 g/t Ag.
summary: London BTC Company staked 36 mineral claims (744 acres) at the Black Star Project in Nevada's Pershing County. Initial due diligence rock chip sampling returned high-grade results: 16.23 g/t gold and 49 g/t silver at the top sample, with five of nine mineralised samples exceeding 8 g/t gold. The project sits 16 miles from Hycroft Mine (16.4M oz Au) in the top-ranked Nevada mining jurisdiction. The company also issued 5.1M new shares to consultants and will distribute 50.7M EBT shares to directors and officers, with share capital enlarging to 363.96M shares.
retail_take: Exploration upside supports the company's gold diversification strategy alongside Bitcoin operations, though early-stage surface samples carry typical sampling bias; administrative share issuance is routine housekeeping ahead of AGM.

headline: FY26 July Trading Update
brief: Barratt Redrow delivers 17,667 homes in FY26 with solid profit; announces £400m shareholder return via buybacks in FY27.
summary: Barratt Redrow completed 17,667 homes in FY26 (vs 16,826 prior year), delivering adjusted profit in line with market expectations despite challenging conditions. The company maintains net cash of £772m and will return £400m to shareholders in FY27 primarily through share buybacks (£386m) plus £14m nominal dividend, capitalising on a 36% discount to tangible net asset value (433.4p per share). FY27 guidance projects 17,700-18,200 completions with 3-4% build cost inflation; administrative expenses expected around £360m versus previous £400m guidance.
retail_take: Solid operational delivery offset by muted FY27 outlook reflecting market weakness; large buyback signals management confidence in valuation but acknowledges challenging near-term trading environment. Likely received positively by income-focused investors shifting to buybacks.

headline: Statement Regarding Shareholder Consultation
brief: Topps Tiles reports shareholder opposition to AGM resolution with 46.84% voting against.
summary: Following the 15 January 2026 AGM, resolution 13 received only 53.16% votes in favour, with over 20% voting against, breaching the UK Corporate Governance Code threshold for disclosure. The Board has initiated shareholder engagement to understand the opposition and will continue consultation on the matter. The company has not specified the resolution's subject matter.
retail_take: Likely negative signal: material shareholder dissent on an unspecified resolution suggests governance or strategic concerns, though absence of detail limits market interpretation and risk assessment.

headline: Calculation of the Tender Price and Issue Price
brief: Investment Company PLC completes tender offer and fundraising; new manager Dowgate Wealth appointed; NAV 77.97p per share.
summary: Investment Company PLC has calculated NAV at 77.97p per share (14 July 2026). Tender Price set at 74.07p (5% discount); Issue Price at 76.41p (2% discount). Company raised £7.16m from placing and offer for subscription. 5.44m shares (59.2% of issued capital) tendered; 3.65m treasury shares and 277,448 new shares issued. Total voting rights post-completion: 13.12m shares. Dowgate Wealth Limited appointed as new portfolio manager with focus on scarcity-themed assets (equities, gold, Bitcoin, inflation-protected instruments). Admission expected 28 July 2026.
retail_take: Successful refinancing with third-party validation via fundraising and established manager appointment. New investment strategy pivot from UK small-cap to thematic scarcity assets represents material repositioning; execution risk remains but provides clear exit option for departing shareholders via tender.

headline: Placing to raise £450,000 & total voting rights
brief: Tern PLC raises £450k via placing of 50m shares at 0.90p, a 31% discount to prior close.
summary: Tern PLC has raised £450,000 before expenses through a placing of 50,000,000 new ordinary shares at 0.90p per share, representing 5.5% dilution post-admission. The issue price reflects a 31% discount to the 1.30p mid-market closing price on 14 July 2026. Net proceeds will support portfolio companies, particularly a potential convertible loan investment in Talking Medicines Limited, and general corporate expenses. Admission to AIM is expected around 20 July 2026, with total voting rights post-admission at 905,543,681 shares.
retail_take: Significant dilution combined with steep 31% discount signals financial pressure and weak shareholder negotiating position, likely viewed negatively despite capital raise for portfolio support.

headline: Annual Results for the year ended 31 March 2026
brief: Yellow Cake reports 39% increase in uranium holdings value to USD2.1bn as spot prices surge 30% amid structural supply deficit.
summary: Yellow Cake's net asset value reached USD2,109.6m (GBP6.33/share) as at 31 March 2026, up from USD1,414.4m (GBP5.05/share) prior year. Uranium holdings increased to 23.11m lb from 21.68m lb, with spot prices rising 30% to USD83.95/lb. The company raised USD283.1m through share placings and exercised Kazatomprom options. Post-year-end, committed purchases bring holdings to 24.37m lb. Profit after tax was USD419.5m versus prior-year loss of USD469.2m, reflecting fair value gains on uranium inventory.
retail_take: Strongly positive read: substantial NAV growth driven by uranium price appreciation, expanded physical inventory, and successful capital raises position the company to benefit from structural nuclear demand growth amid supply constraints.

headline: Warrant Exercise and TVR
brief: Pulsar Helium exercises 43,478 warrants at £0.23/share, raising £10k cash and increasing share count to 214.1m.
summary: On July 14, 2026, Pulsar Helium Inc. (AIM: PLSR) exercised 43,478 share purchase warrants issued in August 2025 at £0.23 per share, generating £9,999.94 in cash proceeds. The new shares will be admitted to AIM around July 20, 2026, and will rank pari passu with existing shares. Post-admission, total voting shares outstanding will be 214,110,225. Pulsar is a primary helium exploration company with its flagship Topaz Project in Minnesota demonstrating 7-8% helium concentrations.
retail_take: Routine capital raise via warrant exercise; minimal dilution impact and modest cash generation suggest non-event for equity holders, though demonstrates ongoing financing activity.

headline: Proposed equity issue to fund acquisitions
brief: Supermarket Income REIT raises £100m via equity offering to fund £216m acquisition of nine grocery assets.
summary: Supermarket Income REIT (LSE: SUPR) proposes £100m equity raise through accelerated bookbuild, South African placing and retail offer to acquire nine UK grocery assets valued at £216m (three immediate purchases for £118m completing September 2026, plus six pipeline assets for £98m). Assets comprise well-let supermarkets with 6.6% average NIY and 10-year WAULT; combined deployment expected accretive to earnings from year one with minimal NTA dilution and improved EPRA cost ratio. Issue conditional on shareholder approval at General Meeting expected 3 August 2026.
retail_take: Accretive acquisition pipeline at attractive yields supports SUPR's stated doubling ambition and aligns with internalised management's strategic progress, though shareholder approval and market conditions remain execution risks. Trading at NAV premium.

headline: Investment Update and 30 June 2026 NAV
brief: Baker Steel Resources Trust NAV falls 13.6% in June to 158.2p amid Middle East volatility and operational issues at portfolio company Futura.
summary: NAV per share decreased 25.0p to 158.2p at 30 June 2026, down 13.6% from 29 May, driven by falling listed metals/mining shares and unlisted holdings revaluations. Despite June headwinds, H1 2026 NAV rose 15.4% versus MSCI World Metals & Mining Index +10.2%. Futura Resources equity valuation cut 31.2% after cyclonic flooding disrupted Australian coal production and delayed Wilton mine restart to Q1 2027; Cemos reduced 10.4% following Moroccan cement sector derating; Kanga Potash increased 100% on equity raise. Trust completed 1.17m share buybacks at 116.4p average; plans maiden interim dividend in September.
retail_take: Mixed picture: strong H1 outperformance offset by June losses tied to geopolitical shocks and operational misfortune at major holding Futura, though management signals recovery potential and shareholder returns via dividends and buybacks.

headline: Results for the year ended 31 March 2026
brief: Eco Animal Health delivers 10% revenue growth to £87.5m with 16% adjusted EBITDA increase to £8.5m; first vaccine approved and launched.
summary: FY2026 revenue reached £87.5m (2025: £79.6m), up 10% or 14% constant currency. Gross margin expanded to 49% (2025: 45%), driving adjusted EBITDA to £8.5m, up 16% and 5% ahead of consensus. North America grew 22% (28% constant currency), Latin America 15%. First vaccine ECOVAXXIN® MS received EU approval December 2025 and successfully launched July 2026. Profit before tax rose 35% to £5.4m; EPS increased 31% to 3.26p. Net cash £25.4m maintained. Up to 9 products expected for approval over 5-6 years.
retail_take: Strong operational delivery with margin expansion, first vaccine commercialisation underway, and robust cash generation suggest positive momentum; China weakness and tariff headwinds offset by geographic diversification and pipeline potential.

headline: PDMR Notification
brief: Living REIT investment manager's entities acquire 577k shares, signaling management alignment with the REIT.
summary: Atrato Services Limited, a person closely associated with PDMRs Benedict Green and Steven Windsor (directors of investment manager Atrato Partners), purchased 577,004 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at £0.776 per share. Combined with existing holdings, Atrato's owners, employees and affiliates now hold 4,360,570 shares (1.11% of issued capital). The announcement emphasizes this demonstrates genuine alignment between the investment manager and the company, reinforcing commitment to shareholder value.
retail_take: Positive signal: investment manager's material share purchase by senior directors demonstrates confidence in Living REIT's prospects and aligns their interests directly with shareholders, reducing agency concerns.

headline: BOARD CHANGES
brief: Sealand Capital Galaxy appoints Mu (Daphne) Zhang as Independent Non-Executive Director and Deputy Chair with immediate effect.
summary: Daphne Zhang, a UK-based capital markets professional with 18+ years' experience advising on IPOs, M&A and cross-border transactions, joins Sealand's board. She previously worked at Panmure Liberum and Grant Thornton on Main Market and AIM deals, and in Beijing advising Asian companies on fundraisings and US IPOs. Zhang holds a Master's in Finance from University of Southampton and ICAEW Corporate Finance qualification. Her appointment strengthens independent governance as the technology investor pursues growth in UK and Asia.
retail_take: Board bolstered with experienced capital markets operator; appointment signals intent to enhance investor communications and execution on international expansion strategy. Market likely views positively as governance improvement with relevant Asia-UK cross-border expertise.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Apertura Energy appoints Chris Steele, former Chevron director, as Non-Executive Director to advance Venezuela energy sector strategy.
summary: Apertura Energy PLC has appointed Chris Steele as Non-Executive Director effective immediately. Steele is an American geologist with 43 years at Chevron, most recently as Director of Commercial (E&P Americas) leading Venezuela sanctions efforts. His expertise includes direct experience with PDVSA, regulatory knowledge of Venezuela's energy sector, and senior roles across Latin America, Africa and the Middle East. The appointment aims to strengthen the Board as Apertura pursues opportunities in Venezuela's energy sector reactivation.
retail_take: Board bolstered with credible Venezuela energy sector expertise at critical execution phase; signals serious intent but Venezuela's geopolitical/sanctions risks remain material for investors.

headline: Commencement of Formal Sale Process
brief: NextEnergy Solar Fund commences formal sale process via Rothschild & Co after years of NAV discount and capital-raising challenges.
summary: NextEnergy Solar Fund Limited has initiated a formal sale process for the entire company following a strategic review. The board, supported by investment manager NextEnergy Capital IM Ltd, cited persistent share price discounts to NAV over several years and challenges raising equity capital as drivers. No active discussions or approaches are currently underway. Rothschild & Co is managing the process; interested parties must sign NDAs and enter standstill agreements. The Takeover Panel granted dispensations allowing confidential participation. The company has 575.2m ordinary shares outstanding and will update markets on process timings.
retail_take: Likely negative short-term for equity holders given persistent discount to NAV; formal sale process indicates board sees external ownership as better value-creation path than standalone listed status, signalling limited confidence in equity market valuation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HVPE purchased 7,000 own shares at £34.032 per share for cancellation on 14 July 2026.
summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited bought back 7,000 ordinary shares at an average price of £34.032 per share on 14 July 2026, pursuant to authority granted at its July 2025 AGM. Following this transaction, the company has 69,640,352 shares in issue.
