The FTSE 250 investment company, which manages a diversified global private equity portfolio, continues its share cancellation programme.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within existing AGM authority; modest transaction size suggests ongoing capital management rather than significant strategic signal; neutral market read.

headline: Production Report for 2Q 2026 & Trading Update
brief: Ferrexpo reports 2Q production of 963kt but faces severe financial constraints; cash runway extends only to early Q4 2026.
summary: Ferrexpo produced 963,000 tonnes in 2Q 2026 (860kt pellets, 103kt concentrate), up 63% q-o-q but down 54% y-o-y. Net cash position deteriorated to US$21m (30 June 2026) from US$25m (31 March 2026) and US$101m (31 December 2024). VAT refunds from Ukrainian authorities suspended since March 2025, leaving US$90.4m outstanding receivables. Company forecasts sufficient cash to operate until early Q4 2026 contingent on iron ore prices, energy costs, and no operational disruptions. Board pursuing minimum US$100m equity raise as primary funding solution; FY25 audited results delayed pending fundraise completion.
retail_take: Highly distressed situation; liquidity crisis with only 2.5-month cash runway creates acute existential risk. Equity dilution likely if fundraise succeeds; total loss possible if it fails. Market will read as severe negative.

headline: Q2 2026 PRODUCTION REPORT
brief: Antofagasta reports steady Q2 copper output with cost pressures; maintains full-year guidance.
summary: Antofagasta produced 142,000 tonnes of copper in Q2 2026, down 0.7% QoQ, with YTD production of 285,000 tonnes down 9.5% YoY. Cash costs before by-product credits increased to $2.94/lb (Q2) from $2.77/lb (Q1), driven by higher diesel, sulphuric acid, and labour settlement costs. Net cash costs of $1.36/lb reflected lower gold pricing. Full-year copper guidance unchanged at 650,000-700,000 tonnes. Major growth projects on track for 2027 commissioning; $0.9 billion Zaldívar water supply investment approved, extending mine life to 2051.
retail_take: Steady production but rising input costs offset by strong commodity prices and unchanged guidance should support investor confidence, though margin pressure from inflation warrants monitoring.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Ruffer Investment Company repurchased 400,000 of its own shares at GBP 2.93 per share for treasury.
summary: Ruffer Investment Company Limited repurchased 400,000 Redeemable Participating Preference Shares on 14 July 2026 at GBP 2.929704 per share, representing 0.104% of issued shares. The shares are held in treasury. Post-transaction, the company has 384,017,764 shares in issue, with 90,354,714 held in treasury. For FCA disclosure purposes, 293,663,050 voting rights are in circulation.
retail_take: Routine share buyback under existing shareholder authority; modest scale and treasury holding are typical capital management activity with minimal direct market implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Grafton Group repurchases 60,000 shares at £8.94 average as part of £25m buyback programme launched 30 June.
summary: On 14 July 2026, Grafton Group purchased 60,000 ordinary shares for cancellation via Goodbody Stockbrokers at a volume-weighted average price of £8.9426, with prices ranging from £8.8570 to £9.0170. This brings total shares repurchased under the £25m buyback programme announced 30 June 2026 to 659,174 shares. Transactions executed across London Stock Exchange between 08:06 and 16:27 BST in 156 trades.
retail_take: Share buybacks are neutral-to-modestly positive signals, indicating management confidence in valuation and capital efficiency, though execution details reveal routine programme progression with no surprises or material accelerations.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
brief: India Capital Growth Fund purchases 200,000 treasury shares at 170p per share on 14 July 2026.
summary: India Capital Growth Fund Limited bought back 200,000 ordinary shares of 1p each at 170p per share for treasury on 14 July 2026. Following the transaction, voting shares in issue total 65,172,624 (excluding 47.3m treasury shares held). The fund, which manages £118.6m in total assets with an average annual return of 12.23% since 2011, is now commencing interim dividend payments from October 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback disclosure for treasury holdings; demonstrates capital management but no material impact. Standard regulatory notification with voting rights denominator update.

headline: Q2 2026 Operations and Financial Updates
brief: PetroTal reports Q2 production of 12,557 bopd, 3% ahead of budget; sells drilling rig for $13.4m.
summary: PetroTal averaged 12,557 barrels of oil per day in Q2 2026 and 13,726 bopd in H1 2026, tracking 3% ahead of budget. The company sold its Amazonia-1 drilling rig for $13.4 million net cash proceeds, expecting a $10 million impairment charge. Cash position strengthened to $136.8 million ($105.3 million unrestricted) from $128.1 million at Q1 end. Management confirmed annual production guidance of 12,000 bopd and plans to resume development drilling in October 2026.
retail_take: Solid operational performance and improved liquidity support market sentiment, though drilling rig impairment and upcoming workover downtime warrant attention. Development drilling restart positioned as key 2027 catalyst.

headline: Update on proposed RTO and General Update
brief: Vast Resources advances RTO of Gulf International Minerals; due diligence near-complete, Tajikistan government approval received.
summary: Vast Resources reports significant progress on its reverse takeover of Gulf International Minerals Limited, with due diligence near-completion and formal confirmation from Tajikistan's government regarding organisational changes at Aprelevka. New exploration data from Soviet Tailings Facilities shows mineralised inventory of 6,645-7,358kt at 1.10-1.40 g/t gold and 304-376 g/t silver. Metallurgical testing indicates silver concentration of 1.095-1.132%. Company received three non-binding term sheets for debt/off-take facilities; equity fundraise requirement narrowed to GBP 5-12.5 million. Extended long-stop date is 31 July 2026.
retail_take: Progress update suggests deal momentum but completion remains conditional on securing debt facilities and placing equity. Resource estimates lack JORC certification and bulk density testing, limiting valuation clarity. Potential positive if financing secured.

headline: Share Buyback
brief: EKF Diagnostics completes buyback of 150,000 shares at 25.5p per share; shares to be cancelled.
summary: EKF Diagnostics Holdings plc purchased 150,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 25.5 pence per share, totalling approximately £38,250. The shares will be cancelled, reducing total issued share capital from 430,273,112 to 430,123,112 ordinary shares. The company executes buybacks as part of capital allocation strategy, with transactions executed across three trades on AIM between 15:38 and 16:02.
retail_take: Modest capital return to shareholders through buyback execution; suggests management confidence in valuation but minimal material impact given share count reduction of 0.03%. Routine capital management activity.

headline: AGM Trading Update
brief: Bloomsbury Publishing confident in delivering record profit for 2026/27, backed by strong title pipeline and Harry Potter TV series launch.
summary: The independent publisher reported strong trading in the first four months of 2026/27, supported by new releases from bestselling authors including Sarah J. Maas, Gillian Anderson, and Peter Frankopan. The Christmas launch of HBO's Harry Potter TV series will drive reader engagement. The company continues benefiting from AI licensing revenue, while the Academic & Professional Division achieved growth across all territories excluding AI. The Board remains confident in delivering Adjusted Profit in line with market consensus expectations of £49.9m revenue of £354.2m for the year ending 28 February 2027, with interim results due in October 2026.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively; company flagging record profit delivery on track with strong consumer titles pipeline and windfall from Harry Potter IP revival, supporting confidence ahead of AGM.

headline: Q2 and First Half Year Results ended 30 June 2026
brief: CelLBxHealth reports H1 2026 revenue of £0.6m; Q2 revenue doubled Q1, burn rate cut 61%.
summary: CelLBxHealth reported H1 2026 revenue of £0.6m (H1 2025: £0.8m) with Q2 revenue of £0.4m doubling Q1's £0.2m. Operating loss improved significantly to £2.7m from £9.7m year-on-year. Restructuring reduced annualised operating costs to £6.3m with monthly burn of £0.3-0.4m. Cash position of £2.9m provides runway to Q2 2027. Company secured Master Services Agreement with AstraZeneca and collaborations with AdventHealth, Royal Marsden NHS Trust, and strategic partnerships with QIAGEN, Roche, Illumina and Myriad. FY2026 revenue guidance raised to at least £2.1m (50% growth). CEO Peter Collins departed 6 July; Executive Chairman Jan Groen leading recruitment.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum with doubled Q2 revenue, dramatically improved burn rate, and major pharma partnerships suggest turnaround trajectory, but material uncertainty on Q2 2027 funding and CEO transition create execution risk.

headline: FIRST QUARTER BUSINESS UPDATE
brief: Workspace reports modest Q1 progress with occupancy gains and £12.6m asset sales; considers additional £100m+ disposals.
summary: For Q1 ending 30 June 2026, Workspace completed 264 lettings and 111 renewals generating £11.9m rental value. Stabilised portfolio occupancy increased 0.6pp to 82.3% with rent per sq ft up 0.9% to £46.87, driving 1.9% rent roll growth to £110.2m. Total portfolio occupancy rose 0.3pp to 79.8%. The company sold £12.6m assets in Q1, bringing total disposals to £138.4m against a £200m original FY27 target, with over £200m currently marketed and consideration of additional £100m+ disposals. Net debt decreased £18m to £740m; proforma LTV remains 35%.
retail_take: Incremental occupancy and rent growth suggest stabilisation but remain modest, while aggressive asset sales signal aggressive balance sheet deleveraging. Execution risk persists on achieving additional £100m+ disposals and maintaining momentum amid softer enquiry-to-letting conversion.

headline: Q2 and H1 2026 Production and Sales Update
brief: Anglo Asian Mining reports record H1 2026 copper production of 8,840 tonnes as Demirli ramps up on schedule.
summary: Anglo Asian Mining produced 8,840 tonnes of copper in H1 2026 versus 1,188 tonnes in H1 2025, with Q2 copper output reaching 5,129 tonnes. Gold production was 12,329 ounces and silver 92,855 ounces in H1 2026. The company generated $125.9m in concentrate sales and increased net cash by $39.9m to $57.6m in Q2 alone. Demirli mine ramp-up continues on track for completion in Q3 2026. Full-year 2026 guidance remains 20,000-25,000 tonnes copper, 28,000-33,000 ounces gold, and 170,000-210,000 ounces silver.
retail_take: Strong operational execution with Demirli ramp-up progressing as planned and substantial cash generation should support equity investors, though the update largely confirms previously guided expectations.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: CT Healthcare Trust buys back 22,977 shares at 134.37p per share for treasury.
summary: CT Healthcare Trust PLC purchased 22,977 of its ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at an average price of 134.37p per share via J.P. Morgan Securities on the LSE. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 268.5m treasury shares with 46.7m shares remaining in issue, representing a modest capital management activity.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury; modest scale with no material impact on capital structure. Signals slight share count management but doesn't suggest major capital allocation shift.

headline: Q1 FY27 Trading Statement
brief: B&M reports 2% group revenue growth in Q1 FY27, with France strong but UK garden season weakness persists.
summary: B&M achieved 2.0% group revenue growth in Q1 (£1,433m), driven by B&M France's strong 14.6% growth and Heron Foods' 2.8% increase. B&M UK revenue rose 0.3% but like-for-like sales fell 2.3% due to weak garden/outdoor performance against prior year's exceptional +10.9% growth. General merchandise returned to growth in May-June. UK FMCG margins remained pressured from competitive pricing, though general merchandise margins improved. B&M France accelerated to +5.3% LFL sales with mid-single-digit transaction volume growth. The company appointed Atheeq Akbar as CFO effective February 2027.
retail_take: Mixed signals: France momentum and GM margin recovery are encouraging, but UK core FMCG weakness and reliance on seasonal garden sales for growth suggest structural challenges remain despite 'Back to B&M Basics' recovery plan.

headline: Infill drilling initiated at Kallak
brief: Beowulf Mining initiates infill drilling at Kallak iron ore project in Sweden to upgrade resource confidence.
summary: Beowulf Mining has started an infill drilling campaign at Kallak in northern Sweden, with an initial 1,000-metre programme comprising seven holes, potentially extending to 1,500 metres pending financing closure announced 7 July 2026. The drilling targets near-surface Inferred resources in Kallak North to convert them to Measured and Indicated categories for inclusion in future mineral resource estimates and pre-feasibility studies. Initial drill hole KAL26008 reached 174 metres and encountered strong magnetite mineralisation at target depths, with results indicating extensive mineralisation throughout.
retail_take: Positive development signal; de-risking early-stage mine economics through near-surface resource conversion should improve project fundability and timeline credibility, though financing-contingent execution risk remains.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Experian repurchased 224,978 shares on 14 July 2026 at 2,583-2,674p, weighted average 2,644p.
summary: Under its June 2026 share repurchase programme, Experian purchased 224,978 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange via Goldman Sachs International on 14 July 2026. The price range was 2,583-2,674 pence per share with a weighted average of 2,644.1172 pence. All purchased shares will be cancelled. Weekly transaction updates will be published on the company website.
retail_take: Routine capital management; share buybacks typically neutral or slightly positive as they can support earnings per share, though market impact depends on execution price relative to fair value.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pantheon International repurchased 293,337 shares at ~390p each on 14 July, intending to cancel them.
summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 293,337 ordinary shares (6.7p nominal value) on the London Stock Exchange on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 389.0p to 391.0p, with a weighted average of 389.99p per share. The shares will be cancelled. Following the transaction, 395,638,556 ordinary shares remain in issue with total voting rights of 395,638,556.
retail_take: Share cancellation reduces share count and supports per-share metrics; modest buyback signals management confidence but represents routine capital management with no material impact.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: M.P. Evans cancels 2,755 shares purchased at average 1,594.74p; total shares in issue now 52.17m.
summary: M.P. Evans Group PLC repurchased and cancelled 2,755 of its own shares on 14 July 2026 via broker Cavendish Capital Markets. Shares traded between 1,560p and 1,614p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1,594.74p. Following cancellation, the company has 52,167,068 shares in issue. All shares carry equal voting rights with no treasury holdings.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; indicates capital management but immaterial volume (0.005% of shares). No significant market implications; standard buyback disclosure.

headline: Launch of employee health screening service
brief: EDX Medical launches UK employee health screening service with agreements covering 14,000 staff and £2m+ FY revenue potential.
summary: EDX Medical Group plc is launching a corporate health and wellbeing screening service featuring biomarker testing panels covering 40-80 markers and 100+ disease conditions. The company has reached agreements in principle with three major UK employers representing 14,000 employees to commence testing in Q4 2026. Management estimates current agreements could generate over £2 million revenue this financial year, with repeat testing expected in future years. The service addresses UK workplace sickness absences costing £130 billion annually.
retail_take: Tangible near-term revenue opportunity with named customer commitments and Q4 start date provides concrete near-term catalyst; however, dependency on finalising documentation and execution risk should temper expectations.

headline: H1 2026 Corporate Update
brief: Iofina posts record H1 iodine output of 393.3MT (+29% YoY), beating guidance. IO#12 Permian plant on track for Q3 commissioning.
summary: Iofina Resources produced 393.3 metric tonnes of crystalline iodine in H1 2026, up 29% from 305.5MT in H1 2025, exceeding upgraded guidance of 385MT. The IO#12 plant in the Permian Basin, built with Western Midstream Partners, is on schedule for Q3 2026 commissioning and is expected to add 170-220MT annual capacity. A pipeline project for IO#11 should be operational Q3 2026. Spot iodine prices remain at mid-US$70/kg; strong customer demand noted, particularly in animal health sectors. H2 2026 production forecast: 460-485MT. The company is negotiating for additional IOsorb® plants toward its 2,000MT per annum target and securing a US$10 million additional loan facility.
retail_take: Strong operational execution and production beat, with major capacity expansion coming online and robust pricing environment support growth trajectory. Positive catalyst for growth-oriented investors, though dependent on IO#12 ramp-up timing.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Plus500 repurchased 64,990 shares on 14 July 2026 at an average price of 4,260.80p under its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Plus500 purchased 64,990 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 through Panmure Liberum at prices ranging from 4,180p to 4,312.50p, with a volume-weighted average price of 4,260.80p. The shares will be held in treasury. Post-purchase, 69,176,414 shares remain in issue (excluding treasury), with 45,711,963 shares held in treasury. This continues the buyback programme announced 9 February 2026 and commenced 16 February 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under an announced programme. Neutral for equity holders as buybacks at or near current trading prices are capital allocation neutral; no immediate implications for valuation or control.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pebble Group repurchases 100,000 shares at 59p per share under its updated buyback programme.
summary: The Pebble Group purchased 100,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at 59.0p per share via Panmure Liberum, spending approximately £59,000. The transaction was executed under the Updated Share Buyback Programme announced on 9 July 2026. All repurchased shares will be cancelled, reducing issued share count to 139,879,209 with total voting rights of 139,879,209.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; modest scale suggests gradual capital management approach rather than significant shareholder value signal.

headline: FY26 Trading Update
brief: Beeks FY26 revenue growth of 11% to £40m with EBITDA up 18%, backed by record H2 performance and new product wins.
summary: Beeks delivered FY26 revenue of £40.0m (up 11% YoY) with underlying EBITDA growth of 18% to £16.0m and PBT up 13% to £6.2m. ACMRR increased 15% to £34.0m, providing strong FY27 visibility. Key wins include three Market Edge Intelligence customers (major bank, North American exchange, global financial services firm) within months of launch. Exchange Cloud deployments with TMX and Kraken progressing; Kraken and ASX now monthly profitable. Infrastructure investment reduced cash to £5.37m from £7.36m but underpins growth trajectory across all four product lines.
retail_take: Solid execution with record H2 and validated new product monetisation likely viewed positively, though cash burn from infrastructure spending may warrant monitoring. Strong pipeline and recurring revenue visibility support forward guidance.

headline: Full Year Results
brief: James Cropper reports strong FY26 results with 33% EBITDA growth, improved profitability and reduced net debt to £8.1m.
summary: Advanced Materials revenue increased 11.5% to £39.8m with record performance, while Paper & Packaging returned to EBITDA profitability in H2 despite a significant customer loss. Group adjusted EBITDA surged 33% to £8.9m on 3.6% revenue growth of £102.9m. Net debt improved £4.8m to £8.1m, reducing net debt/EBITDA ratio to 0.9x from 1.9x. CEO David Stirling noted positive trading momentum into FY27 with strong order books, though Advanced Materials revenues expected to decline near-term due to hydrogen coatings normalization following strong FY26 performance.
retail_take: Recovery narrative supported by operational improvements, balance sheet strengthening and positive trading momentum, though near-term Advanced Materials revenue decline and Paper & Packaging exposure to structural market headwinds temper enthusiasm. No dividend reinstated signals cautious capital approach.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 2,044 shares at average price of $157.40 on 14 July for cancellation.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 2,044 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $157.10 to $157.80, with a volume-weighted average of $157.3958. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, the company has 148,613,282 shares in issue, excluding 5,431,782 held in treasury. The purchase was authorised by shareholders at the May 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Routine share buyback under existing shareholder authority; modest scale (2,044 shares) suggests ongoing capital management rather than major strategic signal. Likely market neutral.

headline: H1 2026 Trading Update
brief: Hunting PLC maintains FY2026 EBITDA guidance of $145-155m on solid H1 performance; subsea and perforating systems outperform.
summary: H1 2026 EBITDA reached c.$62m (12% margin) in line with 40:60 phased guidance. Subsea secured $63.5m titanium stress joint orders from Guyana; Perforating Systems significantly exceeded expectations. Order book grew to $387m from $358m year-end 2025. Working capital increased to $394m supporting H2 ramp-up. Cash position $(19)m reflects $32.6m share buybacks and $10.1m dividends. Full-year EBITDA guidance $145-155m (13-14% margin) unchanged; year-end cash projected $60-65m.
retail_take: Earnings rebalancing towards higher-growth offshore/subsea segments on track with maintained guidance and improved order book; working capital build and near-term negative cash neutral if H2 recovers as projected.

headline: New Contract Win with European Energy Super-Major
brief: GETECH wins multi-year Globe contract with European oil & gas super-major worth $660k over three years.
summary: Getech Group has secured a multi-year agreement with a European-headquartered oil and gas super-major for access to its Globe subsurface intelligence platform, valued at $660k in annual recurring revenue across three years. This contract brings five of the world's six recognised oil and gas super-majors onto Globe's client base. CEO Chris Jepps highlighted strengthened new business pipeline and growing geopolitical importance of energy security driving exploration demand.
retail_take: Positive indicator of growing ARR momentum and competitive moat with super-majors; reflects market tailwinds from energy security concerns and reserve replacement pressure.

headline: AGM Trading Update
brief: Wynnstay Properties reports strong trading start to FY2027 with successful lettings and lease renewals across portfolio.
summary: The property company's current financial year (ending 25 March 2027) has begun in line with expectations. Wynnstay completed a new letting in Uckfield at increased rent, renewed three leases in Aylesford and Heathfield, and agreed terms on four additional renewals in Norwich and Stevenage, all at or above March valuation estimates. Two vacant Liphook units are now under offer, bringing the portfolio to near full occupancy. First quarter rent collection stands at 99%.
retail_take: Positive operational update demonstrating effective tenant retention, rental growth, and strong lease collection rates ahead of AGM, suggesting portfolio performing ahead of valuation assumptions.

headline: FY27 Quarter 1 results
brief: Zegona reports Q1 FY27 results showing third consecutive quarter of growth with improved leverage and successful refinancing.
summary: Zegona Communications' Vodafone Spain subsidiary delivered Q1 FY27 revenue growth of 2% to €916m, with EBITDA up 5% to €465m and EBITDAaL up 3% to €324m. Broadband lines grew 22k to 2,592k and mobile lines increased 345k to 12,864k. Net debt fell 11% to €3.2bn with leverage improving to 2.33x. A €3.7bn refinancing completed 14 July 2026 will deliver approximately €60m in annual interest savings, reducing annual debt costs from €294m (two years ago) to €170m going forward.
retail_take: Consistent operational growth, deleveraging progress, and successful refinancing demonstrate execution of Zegona's strategy; materially lower future interest costs strengthen cash generation outlook.

headline: Teufelskuppe – Channel Sample Results
brief: Kendrick Resources reports strong channel sampling results from Teufelskuppe rare earth project in Namibia, confirming high-grade continuous LREO mineralisation.
summary: Five carbonatite bodies at Teufelskuppe returned exceptional channel sample results via pXRF analysis. Highlights include 2.94 wt% LREO over 53.5m (TKCH004), 1.42 wt% over 197m (TKCH002), and 1.91 wt% over 84.5m (TKCH003). All five bodies showed continuous high-grade mineralisation with peak intercepts reaching 3.20 wt%. Results support an initial mineral resource estimate targeting ~14Mt of above-ground exposed mineralisation. A third RC drill rig arrived on-site to accelerate resource definition and maiden resource completion ahead of preliminary economic assessment.
retail_take: Strong grades and extensive continuity across multiple carbonatite bodies validate project scale and support accelerated development timeline; positive sentiment likely as data derisks resource definition phase and supports world-class REE project positioning.

headline: Listing of AT 1 Notes
brief: Axis Bank receives listing approval for $100m Additional Tier 1 Notes on India INX and NSE IX exchanges.
summary: Axis Bank has obtained final listing approval from India International Exchange (India INX) and NSE IFSC Limited (NSE IX) for $100 million in 6.875% Additional Tier 1 Notes issued on July 14, 2026. These notes will consolidate into a single series with the previously issued $500 million 6.875% AT1 Notes from June 30, 2026. The listing is regulatory compliance disclosure; notes are not registered for US sale and restricted from distribution in certain jurisdictions.
retail_take: Routine regulatory announcement on debt securities listing in India's international financial centres. Capital raise already occurred; listing approval is administrative completion with no fresh capital implications.

headline: Notice Concerning Change in Specified Subsidiaries
Allotment of AT 1 Notes
brief: Axis Bank allots $100m Additional Tier 1 Notes at 6.875%, consolidating with prior $500m issuance.
summary: Axis Bank Limited successfully allotted US$100 million in 6.875% Additional Tier 1 (AT1) Notes on July 14, 2026. These notes will be consolidated into a single series with US$500 million AT1 Notes issued on June 30, 2026, creating a combined US$600 million instrument. The notes will be listed on the India International Exchange (IFSC) Global Securities Market and NSE IFSC Debt Securities Market.
retail_take: Routine capital raising by major Indian bank to strengthen Tier 1 capital position; demonstrates continued market access and investor demand for AT1 instruments; non-event for retail investors.

headline: Preliminary Results Six months ended 30 June 2026
brief: Bank Muscat posts H1 2026 net profit of RO137.49m, up 9.3% YoY; customer deposits surge 13.2%.
summary: Bank Muscat reported H1 2026 net profit of RO137.49 million, up 9.3% from RO125.82 million in H1 2025. Net interest income rose 2.0% to RO210.49 million, while non-interest income grew 12.7% to RO92.23 million. Customer deposits increased 13.2% to RO11.18 billion and net loans grew 6.2% to RO11.39 billion. Impairment losses improved to RO24.30 million from RO30.16 million year-on-year.
retail_take: Solid earnings growth with strong deposit gathering and improving credit quality signals healthy operational momentum; modest net interest income growth reflects competitive lending environment but offset by non-interest income gains.

headline: PROFIT WARNING
brief: Air China warns of H1 2026 loss of RMB2.1-2.6bn due to elevated jet fuel costs from Middle East tensions.
summary: Air China expects a net loss of RMB2.1-2.6 billion for the six months ended 30 June 2026, compared to RMB1.806 billion loss in the same period last year. The airline cited elevated jet fuel prices stemming from geopolitical tensions in the Middle East as the primary cause, which severely squeezed profit margins despite strong operational performance and increased revenue in the first half. The company achieved substantial profits in Q1 but anticipates an overall loss for H1 2026.
retail_take: Negative read; external cost pressures overwhelming operational improvements. Investors will likely view this as a sector-wide headwind beyond management control, but repeated losses signal structural margin challenges for the airline.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Vietnam Enterprise Investments repurchased 5,132 shares at GBP 740 each on 14 July 2026.
summary: Vietnam Enterprise Investments Limited purchased 5,132 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at GBP 740 per share. The shares will be held in treasury, bringing total treasury holdings to 2,543,056 shares. Following the purchase, shares in issue total 123,687,923, representing the company's voting rights denominator.
retail_take: Routine share buyback disclosure with no material impact signalled; standard capital management activity unlikely to move markets.

headline: AGM Trading Update
brief: Dr. Martens holds AGM; maintains FY27 guidance with on-track strategic execution across all four pillars.
summary: Dr. Martens held its AGM on 15 July 2026 and confirmed FY27 guidance remains unchanged. Trading since the financial year start aligns with expectations. The company is progressing four strategic objectives: driving full-price revenue in UK and DACH markets, launching an innovative sandals range, opening new retail concepts globally, and unlocking operating model benefits. USA (largest market) continues growing with strong wholesale performance; Japan and South Korea performing well; European markets tracking expectations amid challenging consumer conditions.
retail_take: Broadly reassuring interim update with no surprises, but lacks specific metrics or upgrades; market likely to view this as steady-state positive with the USA momentum offset by European caution.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Glanbia repurchases 22,114 shares at €23.70 as part of €50m buyback programme through September 2026.
summary: Glanbia PLC bought back 22,114 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at €23.70 per share on Euronext Dublin via J&E Davy, with shares to be cancelled. This purchase forms part of the company's €50 million share buyback programme announced 25 February 2026 and running through 30 September 2026. Following settlement and cancellation, Glanbia will have 238,953,852 ordinary shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback update within announced programme; signals capital allocation to shareholders but is a non-event absent material programme changes or market concerns.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 16,908 shares on 14 July at 906-925p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Gamma Communications purchased 16,908 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 via Investec at prices ranging from 906p to 925p, with weighted average price of 917.2p. Since programme launch on 13 January 2026, company has repurchased 2.68m shares and intends to cancel them. Post-settlement, 91.08m shares remain in issue with 89.57m voting shares (excluding 1.51m treasury holdings). Company is in offer period as announced 7 April 2026.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution during offer period; share cancellation reduces share count but is non-event absent offer context. Market impact likely neutral unless offer details drive sentiment.

headline: Purchase of Own Shares
brief: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchases 15,000 own shares at 157.60p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited (AIM: ADVT) purchased 15,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at 157.60 pence per share, held in treasury. Cumulative buyback total now stands at 5,218,100 shares under the programme announced 4 March 2026. Following purchase, total voting rights equal 131,907,706 shares, with 5,218,100 held in treasury from 137,125,806 issued shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback continuation suggests management confidence in value at current prices; modest weekly purchase indicates measured capital allocation rather than aggressive action.

headline: Costain extends contract with United Utilities
brief: Costain secures three-year United Utilities contract extension, extending partnership to ten years through 2035.
summary: Costain has been awarded a three-year extension to its Managed Service Provider contract with United Utilities, extending the partnership from 2019 through the remainder of AMP8 and with an option to extend to end of AMP9 in 2035. The team of 120+ professionals has delivered over 900 capital maintenance projects and 9,000 responsive maintenance activities to date. United Utilities is investing £13.5bn in water and wastewater infrastructure during AMP8.
retail_take: Positive signal of contract stability and customer confidence; demonstrates Costain's execution track record in regulated utilities sector, supporting revenue visibility through 2035.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: Galliford Try reports sixth consecutive year of growth with adjusted PBT at top end of £51.4m-£53.4m forecast range.
summary: UK construction group expects full-year revenue growth of circa 3% and adjusted profit before tax at the top end of analyst forecasts (£51.4m-£53.4m range). Average month-end cash increased 21% to £216.2m, with year-end cash of £258.8m and no bank debt. The group completed a £10m share buyback programme and deployed £38.4m in capital allocations. Order book stands at £4.3bn with 90% of new financial year revenue secured across public sector frameworks in water, transport, energy, education and defence.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum, robust balance sheet and substantial forward order book suggest durable earnings trajectory; likely read as confidence-building ahead of September results, though modest 3% revenue growth and margin progression toward 4% target may temper enthusiasm.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GlobalData repurchased 97,946 shares at 74.48p average as part of ongoing buyback programme commenced 23 June 2026.
summary: GlobalData PLC purchased 97,946 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 through Investec Bank plc at prices ranging from 73.10p to 76.70p, with a weighted average price of 74.4810p. The shares will be cancelled. Following this purchase, total voting rights reduce to 745,998,027 shares, with no shares held in treasury. This transaction forms part of the company's Share Buyback Programme that commenced 23 June 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within disclosed programme; modest weekly volume suggests measured capital allocation but immaterial to overall valuation. Non-event for market.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund repurchased 74,735 shares at GBP 4.51 per share on 14 July 2026.
summary: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited repurchased 74,735 USD-denominated ordinary shares at GBP 4.510 per share on 14 July 2026, to be held as treasury shares. Total treasury shares now stand at 6,524,914, with 120,554,609 shares in issue. The total voting rights in the company are 120,554,609 shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback disclosure; treasury share repurchases are standard capital management practices with no material implications for shareholders unless done at materially depressed valuations.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Babcock repurchased 161,880 shares on 14 July 2026 at average price of 1004.31p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Babcock International Group purchased 161,880 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 993.4p to 1015.0p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1004.31p. Since 1 July 2026, the company has repurchased 816,537 shares costing £8.24m. Following this transaction, Babcock holds 13.39m treasury shares with 492.20m shares in issue. All purchases executed via J.P. Morgan Securities plc on London Stock Exchange.
retail_take: Routine capital management; buyback demonstrates management confidence in valuation but signals no new strategic developments. Treasury share accumulation typically neutral for equity holders unless coupled with earnings growth or special returns.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Barings Emerging EMEA Opportunities repurchased 433 shares at 809.20p per share for cancellation on 14 July 2026.
summary: The company purchased 433 ordinary shares of 10 pence each on the London Stock Exchange at a weighted average price of 809.20 pence per share via J.P. Morgan Securities plc. The shares will be cancelled. Post-transaction, the company holds 3,318,207 treasury shares and has 11,492,905 ordinary shares in issue (excluding treasury shares).
retail_take: Routine share buyback for cancellation; modestly accretive to EPS but small scale suggests minimal material impact on shareholder value.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchases 250,000 shares at 134.80p, adding to treasury holdings.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 250,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 through Investec Bank plc at a weighted average price of 134.80p. The shares will be held as treasury stock. Following this transaction, HICL holds 166,095,988 treasury shares, with 1,865,392,073 shares remaining in issue (excluding treasury). The total voting rights denominator for disclosure purposes is 1,865,392,073 shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback within established programme; modest scale and single-day execution suggest orderly capital management rather than market signal. No material read-through for retail investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust repurchased 50,000 shares at 812p each on 14 July, holding them in treasury.
summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust purchased 50,000 ordinary shares (25p each) on the LSE on 14 July 2026 via Investec Bank at an average price of 812.00p per share. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the transaction, the company holds 69,564,709 treasury shares against total issued capital of 195,666,734 shares, leaving 126,102,025 shares with voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management activity for investment trusts. Market likely treats as neutral or modest positive given disciplined capital deployment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: TRIG buyback: 270,000 shares at 73.20p on 14 July as part of August 2024 programme.
summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group Limited purchased 270,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 73.20p through Investec Bank plc under its share buyback programme announced August 2024. Following this transaction, TRIG holds 152,637,374 treasury shares. Total voting rights excluding treasury shares stand at 2,333,325,512.
retail_take: Routine execution of announced buyback programme; share price stable at single execution price suggests orderly market conditions with no material news.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: AEP Plantations buys back 58,479 shares at 164-169p as part of announced programme.
summary: AEP Plantations purchased 58,479 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 164.00p to 169.00p, with a volume-weighted average price of 167.77p, via Cavendish Capital Markets. The shares are held in treasury. Since the buyback programme's announcement on 6 July, AEP has acquired 304,757 shares in aggregate. Following this purchase, the company has 399,762,720 shares in issue, with 16,473,997 treasury shares and 383,288,723 voting shares outstanding.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within announced parameters; buyback at mid-range prices suggests orderly execution rather than opportunistic timing—no obvious market signal either way.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IWG purchased 155,000 shares on 14 July 2026 at average price of £1.8895, bringing total buyback to 39.5m shares since December 2025.
summary: International Workplace Group PLC repurchased 155,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 across multiple venues (LSE, CBOE, Turquoise, Aquis) at a volume-weighted average price of £1.8895 per share, ranging from £1.8750 to £1.9160. This brings total share buybacks under the programme announced 31 December 2025 to 39,545,069 shares. Following this purchase, IWG has 954,743,496 shares in issue (excluding treasury shares). The shares will be cancelled or held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management disclosure. Share buybacks at stable prices around £1.89 suggest orderly execution and shareholder returns, but provide no insight into operational momentum or strategic direction. Standard compliance filing.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables bought back 271,961 shares at €0.7530-€0.7630 on 14 July as part of its March 2026 programme.
summary: Greencoat Renewables PLC executed a share buyback on 14 July 2026, purchasing 271,961 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin via RBC Europe Limited at prices ranging from €0.7530 to €0.7630 per share, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7590. The shares will be cancelled. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,083,665,503 shares in issue, excluding treasury shares. This transaction forms part of the company's share buyback programme announced on 5 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution; buyback programmes are standard practice for listed renewable energy companies seeking to optimise shareholder returns and manage share count, typically viewed as neutral to slightly supportive.

headline: Final Results
brief: Cohort PLC reports record FY26 results with 13% revenue growth to £306.4m and 32% adjusted operating profit increase to £36.3m, ahead of expectations.
summary: Defence contractor Cohort delivered record revenue of £306.4m (2025: £270.0m) and adjusted operating profit of £36.3m (2025: £27.5m), with adjusted EPS up 14% to 61.9p. Order intake reached £314.2m with record closing order book of £618.8m extending deliveries to 2037. Net margin improved to 11.8% from 10.2%. Communications and Intelligence division drove growth, particularly from EM Solutions acquisition. The company tripled its bank facility to £175m and proposes 10% dividend increase. Management targets double-digit earnings growth for next three years with 88% of FY26/27 revenue already underwritten by order book.
retail_take: Strong operational delivery ahead of consensus with commanding order book visibility, growing NATO European exposure, and improving margins likely appeal to growth investors; diversification away from UK MoD (now 40%) reduces concentration risk, but geopolitical dependency warrants monitoring.

headline: ANNOUNCEMENT ON KEY OPERATING DATA OF JUNE 2026
brief: Air China reports June 2026 operating data showing mixed results with capacity and passenger declines offset by load factor improvements.
summary: Air China's June 2026 combined passenger capacity (ASK) declined 6.1% year-on-year while passenger traffic (RPK) fell 2.9%. Domestic routes saw steeper declines (capacity -7.7%, traffic -6.7%), but international routes showed traffic growth of 7.4% despite capacity declining 1.6%. Passenger load factor improved to 83.9% (+2.8 percentage points), with international routes up 7.0 points. Cargo capacity fell 6.3% but load factor increased 2.3 points. Fleet expanded to 972 aircraft, with two new routes launched in June.
retail_take: Mixed signals: demand weakness in domestic markets and overall capacity cuts are concerning, but strong international growth, improving load factors, and higher margins suggest operational efficiency gains and strategic repositioning toward international routes.

headline: Q1 trading statement for the three months ended 30 June 2026
brief: ICG reports Q1 FY27 AUM of $126bn, fee-earning AUM up 10% YoY to $88bn; Europe IX flagship fund on track to close at €12bn.
summary: Fee-earning AUM reached $88bn (up 3% quarter-on-quarter, 10% year-on-year constant currency) with $2.4bn net additions. Fundraising totalled $4.1bn in Q1, led by Structured Capital & Secondaries raising $2.2bn including Europe IX's €2.1bn close (standing at €11bn, targeting €12bn by Q2, 50% larger than prior vintage). Deployment reached $3bn in Q1 (LTM $14.3bn). Dry powder stands at $36bn ($18bn not-yet-earning). Fitch reaffirmed BBB+ rating; BofA appointed joint broker; final FY26 dividend 59.3p payable 31 July.
retail_take: Strong quarter showing organic growth, successful flagship fundraising significantly oversubscribed, and solid deployment activity signals healthy market conditions and investor demand for ICG's strategies.

company: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust updates total voting rights denominator to 90.5m shares.
summary: As at 15 July 2026, BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc had 90,472,743 ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issued capital, excluding 27,456,195 treasury shares. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules 5.6.1. The announcement is a routine regulatory notification required under Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating the share count denominator for major shareholding notification calculations; no material implications for investors.

headline: Appointment of CEO
brief: Tamar Minerals appoints mining veteran Dominic Claridge as CEO; Mark Thompson moves to Non-Executive Chairman.
summary: Dominic Claridge, with 35+ years mining operations experience across Australia, Europe, Asia and Africa, appointed CEO effective immediately. Services provided via MET Mineral Resources Limited at £192,000pa, combined with Head of Exploration Sam Bolton. Mark Thompson steps down from Executive to Non-Executive Chairman (salary reduced from £60,000 to £30,000pa). Two related-party transactions approved by independent directors: MMR arrangement and Pure Reports Limited accounting services (£35,000pa, saving £20,000 annually).
retail_take: Appointment of experienced mining executive strengthens operational credibility for tin/copper explorer; related-party structures disclosed transparently suggest governance discipline, though cost arrangements warrant scrutiny for value-for-money.

headline: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings, Inc. files Current Report on Form 8-K
brief: Sunbelt Rentals completes $1.2bn senior notes issuance across two tranches.
summary: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings Inc. (NYSE: SUNB; LSE: SUNB) completed the sale of $450m of 4.950% Senior Notes due 2030 and $750m of 5.650% Senior Notes due 2036, totalling $1.2bn in aggregate principal. The issuance was previously announced and filed via Form 8-K with the SEC on 14 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine debt capital raise with no material refinancing pressure signals; market likely neutral unless rates context suggests refinancing concerns or growth-funded capex.

company: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 100.2m shares.
summary: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust plc has notified the market of its issued capital as at 15 July 2026, comprising 100,226,997 ordinary shares of £0.01 each, excluding 35,359,197 shares held in treasury. The figure of 100,226,997 shares (excluding treasury holdings) should be used as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA rules.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure establishing the voting rights denominator for shareholder notification purposes. No material implications for investors or trading activity.

headline: Board Changes
brief: Tamar Minerals restructures board: Dominic Claridge becomes CEO, Mark Thompson moves to Non-Executive Chairman, Sam Quinn exits.
summary: Tamar Minerals plc (AQSE: TMR), the tin and copper exploration company, has restructured its board effective 15 July 2026. Dominic Claridge has been appointed Chief Executive. Mark Thompson transitions to Non-Executive Chairman, Brett Boynton joins as Non-Executive Director alongside existing Non-Executive Director Andrew Wilson. Sam Quinn has stepped down from the board immediately. Thompson thanked Quinn for his contributions and counsel to the board.
retail_take: Routine board reshuffle with new CEO appointment; lack of detail on Claridge's background or strategic direction limits investor assessment of significance or capabilities.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: PSH repurchases 37,377 shares at average price of 51.75 USD on 14 July 2026.
summary: Pershing Square Holdings purchased 37,377 public shares through Jefferies on 14 July 2026 at an average price of 51.75 USD. The buyback comprised 25,990 shares on LSE (PSH ticker) at 3,863 pence and 11,387 shares (PSHD ticker) at 51.75 USD. The NAV per share was 77.24 USD as of 7 July 2026. Post-buyback, PSH has 173.9 million shares outstanding, with 37.1 million held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback at modest discount to NAV; typical capital allocation activity for closed-ended funds managing share price discount. No material read-through for investors.

headline: First Trust Global Portfolios Management Limited Announces Distribution for certain sub-funds of First Trust Global Funds ICAV
NBPE Announces Transaction in Own Shares
brief: NBPE bought back 30,000 Class A shares at £14.75 on 14 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited repurchased 30,000 Class A shares on 14 July 2026 at £14.75 per share under shareholder authority granted 11 June 2026, via Jefferies International. All purchased shares will be cancelled, reducing outstanding Class A shares to 40,859,149. The company retains 3,150,408 Class A shares in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share cancellations typically neutral-to-positive as they reduce share count and can support per-share metrics, though scale here is modest relative to issued capital.

headline: Final results for the year ended 31 March 2026
brief: TwentyFour Income Fund reports 6.85% NAV return and 10.81p dividend, beating 8p target despite lower rates.
summary: FTSE 250 closed-ended investment company TwentyFour Income Fund (TFIF) delivered NAV total return of 6.85% for year ended 31 March 2026 versus 13.61% prior year. Full-year dividend of 10.81p per share significantly exceeded 8p annual target despite lower interest rate environment (2025: 11.07p). Net assets rose to £926.53m from £843.79m; company raised £178.2m new funds since April 2025 including successful October 2025 realisation opportunity. Market cap reached £996m as of 10 July 2026. Portfolio includes ABS, RMBS and CLOs; trading at average 1.41% premium. No portfolio defaults since 2013 inception.
retail_take: Modest NAV return offset by continued dividend outperformance and strong capital raising, suggesting consistent income delivery amid market volatility. Expansion into US/Australia and shift to floating-rate positioning supports resilience in 'higher for longer' rates environment.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock American Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 61.06m ordinary shares as at 15 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc disclosed its issued share capital consisted of 61,060,138 ordinary shares carrying one vote per share as at 15 July 2026, with 34,301,167 shares held in treasury. The company notified shareholders that this figure should be used as the denominator for calculating notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1. This is a routine regulatory filing required under Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of voting rights denominator for shareholder notification threshold calculations; no material implications for investors or trading outlook.

headline: Transactions by Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities
brief: Sunbelt Rentals amends director shareholding disclosure; Barbara Clark withholding correction on tax obligations.
summary: Barbara Clark, Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer at Sunbelt Rentals Holdings, Inc., had an administrative correction to a prior notification. On 19 June 2026, 2,819 shares of common stock were withheld at $86.06 per share to satisfy U.S. income tax obligations from vesting performance stock units under the 2026 Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan. The amendment corrects an error whereby 784 fewer shares than intended were originally withheld for taxes.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a technical correction to director tax withholding; no material change to holdings or strategic significance; administrative housekeeping without market implications.

headline: Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
brief: MIGO Opportunities Trust to host investor presentation on 27 July via Investor Meet Company platform.
summary: Portfolio Manager Charlotte Cuthbertson will deliver a live presentation on the Annual Results for MIGO Opportunities Trust plc on Monday, 27 July 2026 at 10:00 BST via Investor Meet Company. The presentation is open to existing and potential shareholders, with pre-event questions accepted until 26 July at 9:00 BST. Investors can register free on the platform or will be automatically invited if already following the trust.
retail_take: Routine disclosure providing access to management commentary on annual results; facilitates investor engagement but signals no material news or corporate action.
